plot: Duplicity is the story of a regular office guy who has a hard time making peace with himself. The film is more about images and allegory than about real life situations. It is primarily based on a short sequence of grotesque events that take place in a surreal world.

title: Thaha Chaina
plot: A poignant musical short film that weaves together evocative melodies and visual storytelling to capture the bittersweet essence of fleeting connections, unanswered questions, and the internal longing that persists when words are left unspoken.

title: Nepal Pyaro Chha
plot: A patriotic drama centered around deep devotion to the homeland, tracking the interconnected lives of passionate individuals who stand up against societal challenges and corruption to safeguard their cultural heritage, unity, and love for their country against modern threats.

title: Anaagat
plot: Suresh is an amateur artist who is married to Vidya, an outspoken and brash housewife. Together with their seven-year- old son Suvid, they seem to lead a normal life. This normality includes arguments regarding the household finances and the separate, independent lives the couple lead. Things start to take a serious turn when Suresh discovers that Vidya is hiding things from him. Suresh's best friend Jayant is of the belief that Vidya is untrustworthy and is most probably having an affair. When Vidya's ex is brutally murdered, Jayant firmly believes that Vidya is involved in this crime. Though Suresh is not convinced of this initially, events start to unfold that firmly lead him to believe that the mother of his son is trying to kill him.

title: Heroz
plot: A fast-paced drama centered around the trials and triumphs of a group of ambitious young individuals striving to overcome systemic hurdles and personal hardships, charting their transformative journey as they pull together to protect their community and prove what it truly means to be a hero in the modern world.

title: Hijo Aaja Bholi
plot: A landmark classic drama that captures the shifting sociopolitical and cultural landscape of a society transitioning into the modern era, exploring the generation gaps, evolving family dynamics, and the contrast between traditional values and progressive ideals through the interconnected lives of its characters.

title: Maya's Bar
plot: A nuanced drama exploring the interconnected lives, secrets, and emotional vulnerabilities of the diverse patrons who frequent a local bar managed by a resilient woman named Maya, uncovering stories of heartbreak, redemption, and the search for belonging beneath the neon lights.

title: Haudey
plot: A coming-of-age comedy-drama that captures the vibrant energy, misadventures, and lighthearted escapades of a group of free-spirited young friends as they navigate the highs and lows of youth, romance, and testing societal boundaries while trying to carve out their own paths in the city.

title: Dhansamphati
plot: A lighthearted social satire that follows a series of comedic complications and unexpected twists when a community becomes obsessed with the acquisition of wealth, highlighting the humorous contradictions and moral dilemmas that arise when fortune becomes the primary pursuit of the characters.

title: Bikram Baral: Barai Kayari Bachaulaa
plot: A visually rich musical short film featuring the soul-stirring vocals of Bikram Baral, capturing a deeply emotional story of longing, love, and the painful realities of separation through a seamless blend of evocative traditional melodies and poignant narrative imagery.

title: Maun
plot: A poignant romantic drama centered around the lives of two speech and hearing-impaired individuals, tracing their unique emotional connection, the beautiful silent language they share, and the internal and external hurdles they must overcome to build a future together in a world that struggles to understand them.

title: Hasiya
plot: An intense, high-octane action thriller that features a fierce female protagonist who takes matters into her own hands to combat corruption, systemic injustice, and criminal syndicates, delivering a fast-paced tale of survival, vengeance, and martial arts prowess.

title: Khatte: The Choice of Freedom
plot: This is a documentary film about long-term street children, living in Kathmandu and two protagonists ex street kid Sushil Babu Chetri and Lalit Shasi who run non-government organisation for children in need. Sushil wants to direct street theatre with street children as main protagonists, trying to show alternative ways how to help them and how to face this never ending problem. But his task turns out not to be as easy as it seems, facing more problems than he expected. Lalit Shasi, despite the fact that there are no street children in his orphanage, wants to safe three street kids from the street by giving them bed, food and safety in his organisation. But he faces the reality of the street and struggle to convince children to choose safety instead of freedom of the street.

title: Maya Ta Maya Ho
plot: A sweeping romantic drama that explores the profound depth and complexities of love, tracking the passionate journey of two individuals who must navigate steep societal expectations, familial opposition, and personal sacrifices to prove that true devotion can weather any storm.

title: Fitkiree
plot: The life of loyal and hard-working police inspector is turned upside down when he faced a road construction case. Searching for the person behind the kidnapping of a businessman led him to the clue to the issue.

title: Janma Janma
plot: "JANAM JANAM" is a Love Story Social family Drama.

title: Gorkha Rachhayak
plot: An action-packed historical and military drama that pays tribute to the legendary bravery and fierce loyalty of the Gorkha soldiers, tracking the intense training, brotherhood, and battlefield sacrifices of a young recruit who rises to the occasion to protect his homeland and uphold a legacy of unmatched courage under fire.

title: April Rush: Tyo Hawa
plot: A beautifully shot musical short film that captures the fleeting, ethereal nature of youth, love, and changing seasons, following a pair of characters as they wander through cinematic landscapes set to the breezy, melancholic rhythm of a transformative spring wind.

title: Utkarsha: Drive Towards Drug Prevention
plot: We have always seen a life after people get into drugs. How about sharing a positive notion with young people and motivate them with some words of influential people who stand tall today because they did not choose Drugs.

title: Producer
plot: Three friends struggle to make a cinema which brings lots of fluctuality in their daily life.

title: Pokhara.com
plot: Travel based musical show.

title: Sun Ko Pinjada
plot: A delicate and symbolic short film that uses the powerful metaphor of a golden cage ("Sun Ko Pinjada") to explore themes of restricted freedom, societal expectations, and the suffocating comfort of material wealth, capturing the quiet emotional struggle of an individual yearning to break free and discover their true identity.

title: Woman Rebel
plot: 40% of the rebel army over the course of a ten year revolution in Nepal were women. Woman Rebel follows the incredible story of one such woman (codename 'Silu') from the jungles all the way to the halls of Parliament.

title: The Korean Dream
plot: "The Korean Dream" is about a migrant worker who sacrificed 18 years of his life in struggle to fulfill his influence in the Korean society. Likewise, the dreams of many migrant workers have ended with disappointments, the dream of main character Therefore it's an attempt to show how human rights have been dominated and exploited under the name of government. Furthermore, it's about broken dreams of those migrant workers that were forced to be in exile or under detention.

title: Afanta
plot: A heartfelt family drama that explores the intricate dynamics of kinship and the enduring bonds of loyalty, tracking a character who returns to their ancestral roots to mediate long-standing conflicts, ultimately highlighting the healing power of forgiveness and the importance of holding one's community together in times of change.

title: Radha
plot: A touching Nepali romantic drama starring Jiwan Luitel and Sanchita Luitel. Directed by Yogesh Ghimire, the film explores the lives, cultural values, and emotional struggles of past generations, capturing the timeless essence of love, family expectations, and societal shifts in traditional Nepali society.

title: Deciphering Me
plot: This poignant story explores the inner world of a rebel who belongs to a ultra-leftist underground outfit, his fate depends on the mercy of the villagers, among whom he was forced to rest because of his fatal injuries, fighting with the State. The villagers accused him of the murder of an innocent native girl. Amidst the turmoil, he experienced life and death in every second, all his fellow combatants disappeared fearing further possible army confrontation and he was left alone with his disturbing thoughts.

title: Changa
plot: A fast-paced Nepali dark comedy-drama (*Changa Chet*) that follows three college friends from different backgrounds struggling with urban poverty in Kathmandu. Desperate to fulfill their ambitions and quick-wealth dreams, they fall victim to a financial scam and end up deeply in debt to a dangerous local gangster. Arrested by a corrupt police officer, the trio must navigate a chaotic, overlapping series of misadventures, local crime, and bad decisions to pay back the mob and find redemption.

title: Moving Mountains: The Awakening
plot: A short docudrama that explores the truth of April 25th, 2015 earthquake with important message of Disaster Risk Reduction from the surviving children.

title: Jaalgedi
plot: When a white tourist is seen for the first time in her remote Himalayan village, Jaalgedi - curious Nepalese girl is distracted from her job causing a catastrophe in her family.

title: Smoke through a spider web
plot: In a small village of Nepal, Sabitri and her husband travels around to make a smoke free stove but gets rejected for being a lower caste.

title: Bardo
plot: Wondering in the state of Bardo (PURGATORY) a man returns his home, followed by an unexpected journey of emotions and spiritual experiences.

title: Mission Paisa
plot: All the character make a mission called Mission Paisa and take a revenge on everyone who sent them to prison.

title: Mero Gaon: My Village
plot: "Join Gyetrul Jingme Rinpoche and award-winning director and photographer Indrani Pal-Chaudhuri...[in] Mero Gaon, which tells the story of the rebuilding of a village in Nepal after the devastating 2015 earthquake."

title: Black and White Photo
plot: An evocative and intimate indie short film that follows an elderly photographer living in a rapidly modernizing city. When he discovers a long-forgotten roll of film containing a single, undeveloped black-and-white photo from his youth, it triggers a bittersweet journey down memory lane. The film beautifully explores the passage of time, the pain of lost love, and how a frozen moment can hold an entire lifetime of unfulfilled promises.

title: Safe Schools: Nepal
plot: "Safe Schools: Nepal" highlights the tragic consequences for children in Nepal, who are exposed to trafficking, early marriage, child labor because their education is neglected in the aftermath of the 2015 earthquake.

title: Hateri
plot: A nostalgic Nepali family drama that captures the emotional complexities of rural village life. The story delves into the interconnected lives of its protagonists as they navigate deep-rooted family feuds, societal judgments, and the sacrifices required to preserve love, honor, and community harmony in a rapidly changing world.

title: Maya Baiguni
plot: A poignant Nepali family drama directed by Subash Gajurel, starring Rajesh Hamal, Dhiren Shakya, and Shrisha Karki. The film explores the intricate trials of unrequited love, ungrateful relationships, and familial betrayal, capturing how deeply personal sacrifices and broken trust alter the core of traditional family units.

title: Bhagya Rekha
plot: A classic Nepali romantic drama directed by Deepak Rayamajhi, starring Rabindra Khadka and Mausami Malla. The film weaves an emotional story around the themes of destiny, love, and family expectations, highlighting the struggles of its young protagonists as they try to navigate social barriers and rewrite their own lines of fate (*Bhagya Rekha*).

title: The Yug Dekhi Yug Samma
plot: A poignant Nepali drama that serves as a modern spiritual successor or thematic homage to the legendary original. The film explores an epic multi-generational love story tested by intense family rivalries, societal divides, and personal sacrifices, highlighting how true devotion can endure across eras—from one generation to the next (*Yug Dekhi Yug Samma*).

title: Himalayan Kingdom of Nepal
plot: An action-packed Nepali masala film starring Rajesh Hamal and Dhiren Shakya that blends high-stakes drama, patriotic pride, and comedy against the backdrop of a nation in transition.

title: Manko Babari
plot: Babari is a hard-working village girl who has all the qualities and charm to be likable but the only problem with her is that she was born on Dark Moon Day "Aunsi" and there is cultural disbelief that those who are born on that day should only marry the person who was born on the same day or else her husband will die.

title: Samaya Ra Paristhiti 2
plot: We are often what we make out of ourselves. Perfection is a beautiful illusion. No society is perfect, none of us are the same. Nothing is too right or too wrong. We all just reflect the circumstances we live. We are our times, we are our experiences. This is our Samaya Ra Parsithiti.

title: Yo Kasto Saino
plot: A heartwarming Nepali family drama that explores complex, unexpected relationships and emotional ties, testing the bonds of love, duty, and traditional societal norms.

title: Ma Byujiyepachi
plot: A touching Nepali short film that captures a profound moment of self-realization and emotional awakening within a family context, exploring how a sudden shift in perspective can rebuild broken relationships.

title: Lachhamaniya
plot: The psychological conflict arising from the disagreement between the protagonist's parents and in-laws concerning her giving away ceremony, and the consequences that follow.

title: Mero Hajur
plot: A classic Nepali romantic drama starring Shiva Shrestha and Jharana Thapa. The film follows a deep, emotional love story tested by societal expectations, family obligations, and tragic sacrifices.

title: Play Pause Stop
plot: A minimalist Nepali indie drama that captures the daily rhythms, existential pauses, and sudden shifts in the life of an urban family, reflecting on how modern life constantly forces people to pause, play, or completely stop.

title: Gaunki Chhori
plot: A poignant Nepali social drama starring Keki Adhikari. The film portrays the struggles, resilience, and emotional journey of a young woman from a rural village navigating societal barriers, family expectations, and the harsh realities of moving to the city.

title: Vismrit: Forgotten
plot: Two frustrated young men decide to attempt suicide with the hope of their re-incarnation in a better life.

title: The Rat Hunters
plot: The Rat Hunters is a documentary film that reflects the true lifestyle of rat hunting community 'MUSHAHAR' from south Asia. It is 5 days long bamboo trading journey on ancient oxen cart. It introduces the real rat eating people of the world whose community name is derived from the fact that they eat rat. The word mushahar is made up of two words 'mush' meaning rat and 'ahar' meaning food, thus the people who eat rat are called mushahar. The Rat Hunter is story of a 60 years old mushahar man who works as a bounded labor for landlord as his forefathers, and shows his hardship of bamboo trading trip.

title: Savitri
plot: Directed by Sujit Bidari and starring Keki Adhikari, *Savitri* is a poignant Nepali short film based on Bhawani Bhikshu's short story *Typist*. It follows a married typist who, feeling an emotional void in her marriage, struggles with her complex psychological and romantic attraction to a co-worker whose voice she hears reciting love poems on the radio.

title: Timrai Lagi
plot: A classic Nepali romantic drama that tells a story of intense love, devotion, and ultimate sacrifice. The film follows two lovers who must battle steep societal divides, parental disapproval, and unforeseen tragedies to honor their promises to one another.

title: The Walking Hospital
plot: THE WALKING HOSPITAL is a documentary that journeys alongside a small group of practitioners trained in an ancient form of Chinese field medicine as they travel to remote outposts helping those in need. The first 25-minute segment begins in the crowded streets of Kathmandu and ascends to remote Tibetan mountain villages in Mustang, Nepal in the foothills of the Himalayas. The doctors battle the elements to offer urgent care to individuals isolated geographically and financially. The film is a story about health, healing and the powerful lessons of generosity that transcend borders and boundaries.

title: Pheri Temro Yaad Aayo
plot: A nostalgic Nepali romantic drama that explores the painful lingering memories of a past love. The film captures the emotional turmoil, heartbreak, and resilience of a protagonist haunted by old relationships while trying to move forward in life.

title: Ajhai Pani
plot: The movie portrays a romance between Kushal (Sudarshan Thapa) and Yunisha (Puza Sharma). It also features Surakshya Pant as Shaili, and Bijay Lama as Yunisha's uncle.

title: Sagun
plot: A classic Nepali drama built around themes of auspicious beginnings (*Sagun*), family honor, and relationship complexities. The film explores the emotional struggles of its protagonists as they try to balance traditional societal expectations with their own personal desires and happiness.

title: Mohani Lagla Hai
plot: A classic Nepali romantic drama named after the iconic song, exploring themes of deep infatuation, love, and traditional family expectations as a young couple fights against societal pressures to stay together.

title: Jetho Kancha
plot: A classic Nepali action-drama directed by Dayaram Dahal and starring Rajesh Hamal, Jal Shah, and Rekha Thapa. The film delivers a traditional masala mix of intense family conflict, rural-urban socio-political struggles, and high-energy fight sequences as an honest protagonist fights to protect his family and community from local corruption.

title: Love is Life
plot: Directed by L.N. Gautam, *Love is Life* is a 2013 Nepali romantic drama that explores the profound emotional ups and downs of modern relationships, highlighting how love brings ultimate meaning, resilience, and purpose to the characters' lives.

title: Melmilap
plot: Melmilap is an honest view into the lives and thoughts of four college friends, two Nepali and two American, as they live and travel in the city and countryside of Nepal during its second set of democratic elections.

title: Anyay
plot: A classic Nepali social drama that addresses themes of injustice (*Anyay*), social inequality, and the fight for righteousness. The film highlights the struggle of the marginalized against corrupt systemic forces and the moral courage required to seek justice.

title: Manmandir
plot: The boy mother died in boy birthday and he's father found another girl just look alike as he's mother

title: Mero Paisa Khoi
plot: Directed by Rose Rana, *Mero Paisa Khoi* is a Nepali comedy-drama that centers around the chaotic, humorous, and desperate search for missing money. The film uses a lighthearted, satirical approach to explore themes of greed, financial struggles, and the unexpected lengths to which ordinary people will go when cash mysteriously vanishes.

title: Maya Namara
plot: *Maya Namara* is a 2002 classic Nepali romantic drama starring Rajesh Hamal and Saranga Shrestha. The film follows a sweeping story of profound love, emotional resilience, and devotion, where the protagonists must navigate severe societal pressures, family opposition, and personal sacrifices to keep their bond alive.

title: Majipa: Birsideu
plot: *Majipa: Birsideu* is a 2016 Nepali short music film built around a deep emotional and melancholic theme. Set against a rich cultural backdrop, the video weaves a poignant narrative of love, longing, and the painful process of trying to let go or forget (*Birsideu*), beautifully blending visual storytelling with its musical score.

title: The Shape of Things
plot: This heartwarming documentary takes you halfway across the world and into Nepal's Srijana School for the Deaf. The film offers an intimate look into the everyday lives of four deaf kindergartners, featuring newcomer Lija Gurung. From her inaugural step into the classroom through her first six months of school, the film follows Lija's journey as she forms friendships, learns sign language, and slowly integrates into her new world. The filmmaker's meditative presence and deep love for the children is made manifest in a tender, compelling portrait of Lija and her classmates, allowing very personal access to this pint-sized world filled with curiosity and exploration.

title: Sahar Jane Kura Sunera
plot: *Sahar Jane Kura Sunera* is a 2002 Nepali short musical film that captures the poignant emotions surrounding migration, departure, and the longing inherent in leaving one's home for the city. Through evocative visuals and a compelling musical score, it explores the bittersweet anticipation and the quiet melancholy of bidding farewell to rural roots in search of new beginnings.

title: Love at First Sight
plot: Directed by Michael Davies and starring John Hurt and Phyllida Law, *Love at First Sight* is a poignant British short film set in a residential care home. The story follows 70-year-old Arthur as he experiences a whirlwind romance and falls deeply in love with a beautiful fellow resident named Ruth. However, the narrative builds to a bittersweet twist when it reveals that Ruth is actually Arthur's lifelong partner, and his "love at first sight" is a recurring daily experience caused by his dementia.

title: Beyond the Waters
plot: Discover what is beyond the waters in a spiritual pilgrimage that reveals we are all connected.

title: Anish Rajbhandari: Changa
plot: *Anish Rajbhandari: Changa* is a 2017 Nepali indie musical short film and official music video produced by Infuse Pictures. Written, composed, and performed by singer-songwriter Anish Rajbhandari, the narrative captures a gentle, melancholic romance themed around the imagery of a kite (*Changa*). It subtly explores the transient nature of young love, emotional drift, and longing through a delicate blend of contemporary acoustics and visual storytelling featuring an ensemble cast including Maya Arzu Takayanagi and Urusha Shrestha.

title: Army
plot: Directed by Sovit Basnet, *Army* is a 2001 Nepali action-drama that explores themes of patriotism, military duty, and personal sacrifice. The film follows dedicated soldiers who navigate intense operational challenges and high-stakes combat to defend their country, while balancing the heavy emotional toll the service takes on their personal lives and families.

title: The Monk: Lama La
plot: Jamyang is a young Buddhist monk who has recently finished his intermediate monastic education. He is now heading towards higher monastery for his higher education. In the course of his journey into the war-torn mountains, he meets a young rebel, Arjun. This is a story of their journey where they form an unlikely friendship that will change both of their lives.

title: Troubled Water
plot: Its water problem in Kathmandu valley.This has become a fable almost small resources have been dried up and up. Main source of water supply fulfilling 20% of total demand. Because rapidly growth polulation and huge underground building construction is caused to dry ponds and stone tap. But sever condition can be improved if plan and policy made by government and implemented them properly. major problem of poor people they can not bought water and no natural source .

title: Awaken Eyes
plot: Awaken eyes is a piteous story about a newly wedded couple who have a hardest time to get physically connected. Sharing the room with the sick mother really makes the groom and the bride reluctant to sex on first night of their marriage as they are frequently disturbed with the sick sighs of the mother. Fighting with the obsession of the biological urge they are finally able to make love the following night but the tragedy strikes when they remain aloof of their sick mother's presence.

title: Yatra Ma Bhetera
plot: *Yatra Ma Bhetera* is a 2000 Nepali short musical film and official music video built around a soulful melody. The project captures the bittersweet emotions of a chance encounter, connection, and the fleeting nature of relationships formed while on a journey (*Yatra*). Through romantic imagery and an atmospheric musical score, it reflects on the quiet longing and memorable impressions left behind when two paths briefly cross before moving in separate directions.

title: Malai Maaf Garidau
plot: *Malai Maaf Garidau* (2002) is a Nepali drama that centers on the profound weight of regret, the struggle for redemption, and the complexities of forgiveness. Through the evolving journey of its characters, the film explores the emotional fallout of past mistakes and the painful, often difficult process of reconciliation between partners and families who are seeking to heal broken bonds.

title: Breaking Walls
plot: To honor the new generation of young women, women who are determined to achieve their goals and be themselves, and who are so ready to take down any barrier.

title: Timber to Tibet
plot: Directed by Mohan Mainali, *Timber to Tibet* is a 2006 Nepali documentary short that highlights the environmental and economic struggles in the remote Bhotkhola region. Driven by poverty, local villagers are forced to breach their own sacred, conservationist traditions by cutting down ancient Himalayan forests to trade timber across the border into Tibet for basic food and essential goods.

title: Tashi's Turbine
plot: Tashi Bista dreams to install a makeshift wind turbine in Namdok, a remote village nestled high amongst the Himalayas of Nepal. Namdok, battered by wind and cold has been in darkness for centuries. Wearing Ray Bans to shield his eyes from the dust and just a leather jacket to insulate him from the bitter cold he surveys the village. He grew up in this region without electrical power. He is determined to bring lights to Namdok in an effort to prove himself to the skeptical village community. Tashi's Turbine is a character driven film that shows the impact of one man's dream for light, in a village waiting for development.

title: Bhuimanchhe
plot: Directed by Nawaraj Budhathoki, *Bhuimanchhe* is a 2017 Nepali drama that explores the unique, psychological profile of a forensic pathologist. The film follows a middle-aged coroner (played by Ramesh Budhathoki) whose routine revolves around dissecting dead bodies, but his isolated life takes a profound turn when he develops an unexpected relationship with a spirited college student and neighbor (played by athlete Sipora Gurung).

title: Chame and Thame
plot: The film is about the single father-son debate for a very small portion of land where Thame dreams those could be merely fulfilled.

title: Nabarsiyeki Jhari
plot: Set in a remote village in Nepal, Nabarsiyeko Jhari (A Silent Monsoon) explores the world of Durga as she struggles to set her twelve year old daughter free from their family profession of prostitution.

title: Aamako Man
plot: Directed and written by Janak Gurung, *Aamako Man* (2018) is a poignant Gurung-community-centric family drama. Based on a story by Manju Gurung, the film deeply explores the emotional endurance, sacrifices, and unconditional love of a mother (*Aamako Man* translates to "A Mother's Heart"). It centers on the struggles of keeping a rural family together amidst financial hardships, personal losses, and the painful realities of younger family members leaving home for better prospects.

title: Surya Ply
plot: Directed by Rajendra Shalabh and featuring Jaswant Dev Shrestha and Amol Sharma, *Surya Ply* (2003) is a Nepali short commercial film. Rather than a traditional narrative movie, it was produced as a prominent and highly memorable narrative commercial for Surya Plywood, structured as a mini-story highlighting the strength, quality, and lasting endurance of the brand's wood products.

title: Sex Ed
plot: *Sex Ed* is a 2015 short musical comedy that packs a humorous punch into its brief, two-minute runtime. The film cleverly takes the awkward, often dry topics traditionally covered in basic sexual health classes and reimagines them through a fast-paced, entertaining musical arrangement to satirize the discomfort surrounding the subject.

title: Where Have All The Smiles Gone
plot: Directed by Anja Strelec, *Where Have All The Smiles Gone* is a Nepali documentary that examines the devastating human cost of foreign labor migration. The film follows the thousands of Nepali workers who leave their homes for opportunities abroad in search of economic prosperity, only to face severe human rights abuses, harsh working conditions, and tragic outcomes that leave their families shattered and the nation questioning the true price of its remittance economy.

title: Dancing on the Fire: Khali Khaney
plot: Running and Dancing on the fire by shamanistic method for the salvation of the soul.

title: Remembering Tarkovsky
plot: A man is enjoying his loneliness, observing things around him. Remembering his memories, desires and thinking of what made of this life in a narrow world he lives in.

title: Chyatiyeko Note (A Day with Rs 100)
plot: Directed by Arjun Subedi, *Chyatiyeko Note (A Day with Rs 100)* is a 2010 Nepali short film that follows a single 100-rupee bank note over the course of a day. The narrative uses the journey of the torn note (*Chyatiyeko Note*) as a lens to explore different socio-economic strata in urban Nepal, illustrating how the exact same bill passes through various hands—reflecting the diverse struggles, values, and daily realities of the people who handle it.

title: Ujwal Bhandari: K dosh thiyo...
plot: Ani has to choose between someone she loves and someone who loves her. Choice is simple but decision is tough. Love finds a new way as she decides to remain unmarried worshiping her life in the name of God.

title: Butterfly (colors of love)
plot: Directed by Om Pratik Gurung, *Butterfly (Colors of Love)* is a 2018 Nepali thriller and romantic drama starring Aaryan Adhikari, Priyanka Karki, and Arpan Thapa. The film juxtaposes the vibrant, evolving emotions of romance with a high-stakes narrative of suspense and adventure, exploring how the different "shades" of love can unravel into unpredictable and dangerous situations when obsession, jealousy, and secrets come to light.

title: Audition
plot: A common guy who is so much passionate and has keen interest to be a superstar in film industry. As per result, he being overconfident , finds acting AUDITION as great opportunity to reflect his talent. Finally he rushes and goes for AUDITION.

title: NOMADS BUT NOT...
plot: what will be the effect upon the Sailung area's biodiversity ? After they change their Profession can they improve their living standard? And what is the present market of Chhurpi (Product of Nuk's milk).Why Chauri is necessary in sailung for ecosystem balance? Why Chauri is important for the preservation of history, culture and rituals of Sailung?

title: Maxx
plot: *Maxx* is a biographical documentary project that chronicles the personal life, artistic development, and career trajectory of its subject. Moving away from highly stylized dramatic reenactments, the film relies on direct personal accounts, historical records, and intimate footage to explore the individual's formative experiences, core motivations, and the defining moments that shaped their professional journey and lasting legacy.

title: Prarabdha
plot: Santosh is an impoverished, pathetic, down and out school teacher who miserably fails in everything in life. One day he tries to commit suicide, and fails even at that! Santosh looks like any other ordinary school teacher, dressed in cotton trousers and shirt, shaved clean with a band aid on his forehead. His eyes have a look of sadness - speaking of a life spent in abject poverty and a cold resentment against a cruel, heartless society. But today, for some undisclosed reason, his spirits are high and he looks happier than usual.

title: The Knot
plot: A story about husband and wife, unfolding their past and present during the shopping for the festival 'Dashain'.

title: Beneath Everest: Nepal Reborn
plot: From deep in the Himalaya mountains where the Maoist soldiers greet the morning sun as they warm up, to the cities where a powerful movement for change explodes on the streets, people of Nepal have experienced moments they never thought possible. Filmed over four years during the height of the insurgency and the historic transition of Nepal from a 240-year old monarchy to a Republic nation, 'Beneath Everest' is a journey that exposes the grass root realities of Nepal's ten-year war. The film critically questions the use and continuation of violence and challenges its justifications. Directed by a native Nepali, the film encourages Nepalis silenced by fear to tell their stories, and challenges the to reflect on their fears, triumphs and hopes as Nepal begins the long journey towards peace.

title: Sapana
plot: Directed by Sambhu Pradhan and produced under the banner of Pushpanjali Production, *Sapana* (1993) is a classic Nepali family drama. Featuring a musical score composed by Ranjit Gazmer, the film delves into the emotional complexities of human relationships, personal ambitions, and the societal pressures that test a family's unity, echoing the themes of sacrifice and resilience central to its narrative.

title: Kati Dherai
plot: *Kati Dherai* (2015) is a Nepali romantic short film that captures the innocent, yet intense emotions of young love. The narrative centers on the heartwarming journey of two individuals navigating the initial sparks, sweet vulnerabilities, and deep affection (*Kati Dherai* translates to "So Much") that define their relationship, highlighting how small, shared moments can build a profound emotional connection.

title: So Simple
plot: Directed by Shiva Regmi, *So Simple* (2012) is a Nepali social drama that marks the final directorial venture of the prominent filmmaker. The film explores the intricate emotional trials, romantic conflicts, and moral choices of ordinary people navigating contemporary societal pressures, anchoring its narrative on the core theme that life's most profound answers are often found in simplicity.

title: They Call It Love
plot: "They call it Love" tells the story of two couple we movie in despite objection from their family. As they live with each other they discover each other's past lives, which leads to more problems in their lives. It's a simple story told through use of montages.

title: The Outsider's Band: Maili
plot: A vibrant and culturally rich musical short film that tells a poignant story of love, waiting, and rural life through the evocative sounds of folk-rock melodies, following the emotional journey of a young woman named Maili as she navigates the beauty and hardships of her mountain village community.

title: z
plot: A modern man is in search for meaning of his existence in meaninglessness.

title: Raanko
plot: A gritty and intense social drama that delves into the harsh realities of class struggle, oppression, and systemic corruption within a rural community, tracking a resilient protagonist who sparks a revolution (symbolized by the torch, or "Raanko") to challenge tyrannical forces and fight for justice, equality, and the reclamation of human dignity.

title: Premnath
plot: An action-packed drama centered around a charismatic and fiercely loyal protagonist named Premnath, who rises to protect his community from ruthless local syndicates, combining hard-hitting fight sequences with a heartfelt narrative of justice, family duty, and standard heroic redemption.

title: Mahakabi Devkota
plot: Based on the life and work of poet, Laxmi Prasad Devkota.

title: Ajambhari Maya
plot: A sweeping romantic drama that celebrates the concept of eternal, undying love ("Ajambhari Maya"), following two devoted lovers as they confront deep-seated family opposition, tragic twists of fate, and societal pressures, proving that their spiritual connection can endure well beyond the constraints of time and hardship.

title: The Touched
plot: The documentary 'Touched' provides a unique glimpse behind the walls of Maiti Nepal, an institution created by Anuradha Koirala (CNN hero 2011) to give shelter and strength to Nepalese women who escaped the brothels of Mumbai, Delhi and the Middle East. Some of these women were as young as eight when they were forced to work in the brothels, this happens to thousands of girls every year. Three artists from Australia went to Kathmandu to help the women paint the walls of the institution with images of color and beauty. As the mural unfolds, the lives of the women in the hospice is revealed and their stories are told, sharing their attitudes to life and hopes for the future. It is a story of courage and survival in the face of seemingly impossible odds.

title: UnShattered Desires
plot: Just after the devastating earthquake of 25 April, 2015, the district of Gorkha in Nepal was highly affected as the epicenter of the quake was itself was at village of Barpak in Gorkha. It was not easy for the people of the most of affected area to come back into the normal life. Every thing was not in order. Even the government was confused about the way to reach out to the people in effective way. But for the people of Sirdivas village development committee(VDC) in Gorkha, near epicenter of the earthquake, the story was different. They found a way to move out of panic situation and get along to the normal life. This is a film about courage of local people of Sirdibas VDC, a remote area in Gorkha district in Nepal, which was the epicenter of devastating 7.8 Richter scale earthquake on April 25, 2015. This is a story of people coming out of trauma and getting back to the normal life. How they came out of their damaged houses and get along to build a new foot trail of opportunity. This is story of a very noble initiative of a local NGO of Gorkha, UN Nepal to bring back the people to normal life after earthquake. Shot entirely during the aftermath of the earthquake the film is a unique story of courage of villagers living far beyond the signs of development. It is the story about meaning of development to the villagers and how they get along with to develop their village during worst situation they ever faced in their lifetime.

title: Parikshya
plot: Moral dilemma of a witness of a cow muderer.

title: The Palmist
plot: A compelling drama-mystery that follows the enigmatic life of a gifted palmist who possesses an uncanny ability to read people's pasts and foresee their futures. When a mysterious client arrives seeking answers to an unresolved tragedy, it sparks a deeply personal journey filled with forbidden romance, long-buried secrets, and an intense web of deception, challenging the fragile line between written destiny and free will.

title: Kutumberi Saino
plot: A sprawling family and social drama that explores the intricate dynamics of kinship, community relations, and ancestral bonds within a traditional village setting. The narrative delves into the beautiful yet complex web of societal expectations and cultural ties ("Saino") that bind an extended family together, tracking their collective emotional struggles, conflicts over inheritance or modernization, and the ultimate realization that unity, mutual respect, and cultural heritage are paramount to surviving the challenges of a rapidly changing world.

title: Mr. Mangale
plot: A lighthearted, situational comedy that follows the chaotic and humorous misadventures of a quirky and eccentric bachelor named Mr. Mangale. Desperately searching for a suitable life partner, his over-enthusiastic attempts to navigate the modern dating world and traditional matchmaking customs repeatedly backfire, leading to a series of misunderstandings, comical errors, and endearing moments of self-discovery.

title: Kehi Mitho Yaadma
plot: A melancholic and beautifully shot musical short film that delves into the bittersweet ache of nostalgia and lost love. Set against a backdrop of cinematic melodies, the story follows a reflective protagonist as they revisit long-cherished places and quiet fragments of the past, capturing the lingering, sweet memories ("Kehi Mitho Yaadma") of a romance that once was.

title: Gold Coast 2 Ghorka
plot: A poignant drama that explores the challenges, cultural displacement, and emotional journeys of individuals transitioning between two vastly different worlds—the bustling, modern coastal lifestyle of Australia's Gold Coast and the deeply rooted, traditional homeland of the Gorkhas. The narrative delves into the themes of identity, belonging, and sacrifice as the characters navigate the complex realities of chasing a dream abroad while staying connected to their cultural heritage.

title: Soltini
plot: A high-stakes romantic action film that follows the intense struggle of a man striving to win the heart of his "Soltini" (a cousin or close companion often culturally linked to marriage) amidst a backdrop of escalating conflict. The plot weaves together pulse-pounding pursuit sequences and fierce confrontations with internal family tensions, highlighting the protagonist’s determination to overcome societal obstacles and rival threats to secure a future with the one he loves.

title: Yo Raat Behoshi Yo Bata Behoshi
plot: A hauntingly atmospheric musical short film that captures the dizzying, dreamlike haze of emotional heartbreak and existential longing. Set against a backdrop of nocturnal landscapes, the narrative follows a wandering protagonist navigating an intoxicating night, where the lines between reality and memory blur to the rhythm of a melancholic melody, perfectly embodying the song's themes of a dazed night and an uncertain path ("Yo Raat Behoshi Yo Bata Behoshi").

title: Voex
plot: A depressed teen wants to improve his lifestyle and forget his past but then he finds a coin bank shaped like a pigeon who fulfills all his wishes but..

title: Choor: Here
plot: A poignant short documentary that offers an intimate look at the lives, struggles, and everyday resilience of individuals living in a specific community or under unique socio-economic conditions. Through raw, observational storytelling, the film captures the raw essence of "being present" in a particular place and time, shedding light on personal narratives, cultural identity, and the unspoken human connections that define their reality.

title: Bhai Tika
plot: A story about a brother and a sister around the Bhai Tika festival.

title: Gaajal
plot: A passionate musical romance that captures the deep intensity of a fateful love story, utilizing the classic metaphor of "Gaajal" (kohl) to signify the deep, indelible impression that a beloved leaves on one's heart. The film weaves together a rich, melodic soundtrack with a narrative of youthful adoration, emotional vulnerability, and the bittersweet trials that test the couple's devotion as they fight against societal expectations to keep their love alive.

title: The Way To Heaven
plot: Pashupatinath temple is a huge structure that looms over the banks of the Bagmati river, which ultimately joins the holy river Ganges. Hindu cremation ceremonies take place here on an industrial scale. Visitors are invited to observe the rituals which might seem a little strange, but to Hindus death is an important part of the journey of life

title: Aashirbad
plot: An emotionally gripping family drama that centers on the profound power of a parental blessing ("Aashirbad") and the generational sacrifices made for the sake of family honor. The narrative follows a young protagonist who must navigate personal ambitions, complex societal expectations, and severe financial hardships, ultimately discovering that true success and resilience are rooted in the unconditional support, wisdom, and blessings of their elders.

title: K Desh Doshi Cha?
plot: "We reap what we sow. We are the makers of our own fate. The wind is blowing; those vessels whose sails are unfurled catch it, and go forward on their way, but those which have their sails furled do not catch the wind. Is that the fault of the wind? We make our own destiny."

title: Remember Humla
plot: 'Remember Humla' follows American, Charles Atkinson, on his journey to a remote corner of Nepal where his understanding of happiness is challenged when he discovers an ancient Tibetan culture threatened by extinction but continues to carry on their thousand year old traditions.

title: Natedar
plot: A sweeping family drama that centers on the profound bonds, obligations, and intricate dynamics shared among relatives ("Natedar"). The narrative follows a tight-knit family whose unity is severely tested by generational misunderstandings, disputes over traditional values versus modern ambitions, and unexpected financial hardships, ultimately weaving a heartwarming tale of reconciliation, sacrifice, and the enduring power of kinship.

title: Kakhara
plot: A poignant and thought-provoking short documentary that investigates the foundational challenges and systemic gaps within the primary education system, particularly in rural or marginalized regions. By focusing on the literal meaning of "Kakhara" (the basic alphabet), the film captures the personal struggles of young students, passionate educators, and underfunded schools, shedding a crucial light on the vital importance of literacy and equal access to basic learning as a fundamental human right.

title: The Designer
plot: Past, Present, and Future Illusion of choice Coincidence, Faith, Belief and Time Life is designed!

title: A Walk to Manang
plot: A bunch of students make a trek to Lower Manang-Western Himalayan Region of Nepal. During the trek, they discover the myths, histories and the importance of Manang and the trekking trial.

title: A Rough Cut on the Life and Times of Lachuman Magar
plot: A compelling and raw biographical documentary that offers an intimate look into the remarkable life, unique challenges, and socio-political environment surrounding its titular subject, Lachuman Magar. Through an unpolished, "rough cut" observational lens, the film weaves together personal interviews, historical context, and candid footage to explore themes of identity, resilience, and the broader cultural struggles of the Magar community within the shifting fabric of modern history.

title: Daiva Sanjog
plot: A profound drama that centers on the cultural and philosophical belief in destiny and divine intervention ("Daiva Sanjog"). The narrative follows two individuals from completely different walks of life whose paths are crossing by an unexpected twist of fate, forcing them to navigate personal hardships, social divides, and emotional trials while discovering that their lives are guided by a higher, cosmic purpose rather than mere coincidence.

title: The Man Who Died Once
plot: Tragic circumstances bring together the wife and the mother of two migrant workers - one from the east, the other from west Nepal.

title: The Copper Village
plot: At a village in western Nepal where copper mines had operated for 400 years, former workers and local residents gather to reenact the vanishing practice of mining and smelting. The film captures village life and its festivities through the eyes of a visual anthropologist.

title: Melong
plot: A profound cultural drama set against the breath-taking landscapes of the Himalayas, *Melong* (meaning "Mirror" in Tibetan) delves into the spiritual concept of self-reflection, karma, and generational shifts. The narrative follows a traditional family facing an emotional crossroads when ancient, deeply rooted community customs clash with the enticing promises and inevitable pressures of the modern world. As long-buried secrets surface and test their relationships, the characters must look into the symbolic mirror of their own actions, learning that true peace and reconciliation can only be found by honoring their heritage and embracing the truth within themselves.

title: A Mayalu Timi Lai
plot: A vibrant and emotionally resonant musical short film that celebrates the joyous highs and tender vulnerabilities of young romance. Moving to the rhythm of a sweeping melody, the story follows an affectionate protagonist expressing their deep, heartfelt devotion to their beloved ("A Mayalu Timi Lai"), beautifully capturing the universal magic of courtship, shared dreams, and the sweet promises that bind two lovers together.

title: Chaukaith
plot: Chaukaith (Threshold) is about an encounter between two young women from two different worlds.

title: Fever
plot: A mom gets surprised as she discovers her son is having fever for no reason. She gets more shocked to find everyone in her family has fever.

title: Kubaato
plot: "Kubaato" in Nepali means "wrong way." Set in Nepal, a kidnapping plot goes wrong

title: Paani
plot: A boy in Nepal dreams anxiously about the world's water. As he sleeps, he sees it disappear before his eyes.

title: Sanyas
plot: A deeply spiritual and contemplative drama that explores the profound journey of renunciation, inner peace, and the search for higher truth. The narrative centers on a protagonist who, after experiencing an existential crisis or severe emotional heartbreak amidst the material demands of everyday life, decides to leave behind worldly attachments, wealth, and societal expectations to embrace the life of a hermit (*Sanyas*). Set against serene natural landscapes, the film beautifully captures their psychological and spiritual transformation, detailing the trials of isolation, self-reflection, and the ultimate quest for enlightenment.

title: Ma Khushi Chu
plot: Vashna lives to make others happy; their happiness is hers. Aakash is an aimless wanderer, with no ambition other than to live happily in the moment. When the two disparate souls meet, both are forced to rethink the meaning of happiness.

title: Young Torture: Namarosh Hamro Maya
plot: A gritty and emotionally raw musical short film that portrays the turbulent, painful realities of youthful heartbreak and toxic relationships. Moving to a dark and haunting rhythm, the story follows a fractured couple trapped in a cycle of emotional distress and regret, powerfully capturing the agonizing transition from a once-passionate romance into a state of psychological torment and mutual isolation ("Namarosh Hamro Maya").

title: P.S. Zindagi
plot: P.S. Zindagi (Post Seismic Life) is Nepal's first online sitcom. It shows the unfolding of comedy and drama in the lives of five Nepali youth from diverse backgrounds, following the April 25th 2015 earthquake. It was a creative experiment where a small team used their own individual resources and passion to create a fun and self-empowering narrative of the lives of the urban youth.

title: Threads of Hope
plot: A moving short documentary that shines a light on the resilience and resourcefulness of women overcoming economic and social adversity through the art of traditional weaving and textile craftsmanship. The film profiles individuals who turn a shared craft into a powerful vehicle for financial independence, community building, and healing, showing how they piece together their shattered lives and stitch a new future—one thread of hope at a time.

title: Tama Gaun: The Copper VIllage
plot: In Nepal copper has a number of symbolic meanings and economic aspects connected to exchange and healing properties, as well as being the Hindu god Shiva. This film is about the age-old traditional practice of mining, smelting and casting of copper.

title: Oscar Fun Talk
plot: A lively and engaging entertainment talk show special that brings together film critics, industry insiders, and passionate cinephiles to dissect the major nominees and frontrunners of the Academy Awards. Across a dynamic half-hour discussion, the panel mixes sharp cinematic critique with lighthearted banter, offering predictions, uncovering behind-the-scenes trivia, and debating the snubs and surprises that define Oscar season.

title: Pralobhan
plot: It is a story of a girl who has lost her entire family in the massive earthquake of 12 Baisakh, 2072.

title: Hero
plot: A dynamic and energetic musical short film that celebrates the spirit of ordinary individuals rising to extraordinary circumstances. Set to a driving, rhythmic soundtrack, the narrative follows an aspiring protagonist who finds the courage to overcome deep-seated fears, self-doubt, and everyday adversities, ultimately discovering that true heroism is not defined by superpowers, but by the resilience, kindness, and strength found within one's own heart.

title: Saas Fere Jasto Lagcha
plot: Set in the transitional political situation of Nepal, the story hovers around the four Nepalese people and their passion, happiness, madness, fanaticism, fear, anger, isolation, melancholy and each ones struggle to transform their situation by following what is meaningful and appealing to them and experience a moment of euphoria.

title: Red Bull Vs. Monster
plot: After drinking two different energy drinks, a cowboy and a cowgirl begins a chase against each other to prove/disprove the energy supplied by each drink. Ironically, soon later, they begin another chase with new energy drinks.

title: Kumari: The Jagat Lama Story
plot: A profoundly moving biographical documentary that explores the life and enduring humanitarian legacy of Jagat Lama. Set against the majestic yet challenging landscapes of rural Nepal, the film focuses on his deeply personal mission to build the "Kumari" project—a dedicated effort to bring sustainable healthcare, education, and economic empowerment to his remote home village. Through intimate interviews and striking observational footage, the documentary highlights how one individual's vision and resilience can uplift an entire community from the cycle of poverty and geographical isolation.

title: Hridya Chune
plot: A poignant musical short film that explores the profound, unspoken connections between people. Through a series of evocative vignettes set to a soulful soundtrack, the story captures moments of raw emotional vulnerability—the quiet comfort of companionship, the ache of longing, and the transformative power of empathy—illustrating how simple, heartfelt gestures can touch the heart ("Hridya Chune") and leave an indelible mark on the soul long after the music fades.

title: Waiting Room
plot: A compelling and emotionally charged short drama that takes place entirely within the confines of a sterile, high-tension hospital waiting room. As the clock ticks, the film explores the universal experience of helplessness and anticipation through the eyes of several strangers whose paths cross during a moment of personal crisis. By focusing on their shared anxieties, quiet interactions, and the weight of what remains unsaid, the story captures the profound human capacity for empathy and reflection during life's most vulnerable and uncertain transitions.

title: Jindagi Ko Har Mod Ma
plot: A poignant and reflective musical short film that captures the bittersweet passage of time and the unpredictable nature of fate. Set to a melancholic and evocative soundtrack, the story follows a contemplative protagonist looking back on life's journey, illustrating how the choices made, the love found, and the heartbreaks endured shape our destiny at every single crossroad of life ("Jindagi Ko Har Mod Ma").

title: Hundred Rupees
plot: This inspirational short movie is based on truth and reality. The protagonists of this film are stone-grinder making mother and her begging daughter. The main motto of this film is that, though this generation is not secured from the widespread sexual harassment in the society. The education is must to be safe for forthcoming generation.

title: Evening days
plot: youngsters lose their way to darker days...

title: Mani Tamang feat. Subani Moktan: Timrai Lagi
plot: A vibrant and emotionally resonant musical short film that beautifully captures the essence of unconditional love and deep romantic commitment. Moving to a melodic and soulful rhythm, the narrative follows a couple navigating the tender highs and comforting moments of their relationship, highlighting how every heartbeat, creative expression, and shared dream is dedicated entirely to the one they love ("Timrai Lagi").

title: 68 Members
plot: 68 Members is the joint family story from far western terai part of Nepal.

title: Saving Dolma
plot: A documentary about the position of female migrant workers in the Middle East.

title: Betterfelt.com
plot: A captivating short documentary that explores the inspiring intersection of sustainable fair-trade business and traditional craftsmanship in Nepal. The film provides an intimate, behind-the-scenes look at the skilled artisans—primarily local women—who handcraft wool felt products for the Danish eco-conscious brand, betterfelt.com. Through quiet observational footage and personal interviews, the documentary captures how this collaborative enterprise fosters economic independence, preserves indigenous wool-felting techniques, and weaves a vital social safety net for a community striving to flourish in a modern economy.

title: Pahichan
plot: A gripping and socially conscious short drama that delves deep into the universal quest for identity, belonging, and self-worth ("Pahichan"). The narrative follows a marginalized individual navigating a rigid social structure, forced to confront systemic barriers, deep-seated cultural prejudices, and inner psychological struggles. Through intense, quiet moments and emotional confrontations, the film powerfully explores the heavy cost of living authentically and what it truly means to reclaim one's own voice and place in the world.

title: Vedi Gothale
plot: A gripping and emotionally charged social drama set against the rugged beauty of rural Nepal, *Vedi Gothale* (The Shepherd) explores the deeply rooted hardships, pride, and moral conflicts of traditional pastoral life. The narrative follows a dedicated shepherd who faces a severe crisis when changing environmental conditions, modern economic pressures, and a fracturing family structure threaten his ancestral way of life. Forced to choose between upholding his sacred, generation-spanning bond with the mountains and conforming to a rapidly modernizing society, he embarks on an intense psychological and physical struggle to protect his livelihood, identity, and the people he loves.

title: Bhariya
plot: After their parent's death two children run away to city to live better life.

title: Santushti
plot: An evocative and soulful musical film that delves into the profound human search for inner peace, contentment, and spiritual fulfillment (*Santushti*). Through a seamless blend of lyrical melodies and rich visual storytelling, the film follows a contemplative protagonist who begins to look past the superficial distractions and relentless pressures of material life. By turning inward and finding beauty in simplicity, nature, and genuine human connection, the narrative captures a deeply moving emotional transformation, illustrating that true happiness is not found in what we accumulate, but in the quiet fulfillment of the soul.

title: A day in the life of kanchi
plot: A one minute portrait of actress Kanchi's one day of her life who is living in Nepal with her daughter just 7 years while her husband works hard in abroad.

title: The Eternal Journey
plot: This film chronicles ananta yatra (eternal journey) of and old woman who meets sudden demise in the 2015 AD Nepal Earthquake towards her salvation.

title: U Got Me
plot: A sleek and stylish musical short film that captures the electrifying spark of a whirlwind modern romance. Driven by an infectious, upbeat rhythm, the narrative follows the playful back-and-forth dynamic between two people who share an undeniable, magnetic chemistry from the moment they meet. Through dynamic choreography, vibrant visuals, and a captivating melody, the film beautifully expresses the intoxicating rush of realizing that someone has completely captured your heart and thoughts ("U Got Me").

title: Talking to the Air: Horses of the Last Forbidden Kingdom
plot: On a tenuous border with China, the vanishing medieval culture of Mustang is where a man's wealth is measured in horses and riding is as elemental as breathing. TALKING TO THE AIR tells the story of the ascent of civilization in the high Himalaya and turns a lens on issues of globalization, fragile border politics and the precarious future for the horse in this remarkable region.

title: Maya Ko Saino
plot: A sweeping and emotionally resonant romantic drama that explores the intricate, deeply woven bonds of love, family honor, and cultural destiny ("Maya Ko Saino"). Set against the breathtaking landscapes of Nepal, the narrative follows two star-crossed lovers whose deep affection for one another faces severe testing from rigid societal expectations and long-standing generational rifts. As they struggle to honor their hearts without tearing their families apart, the film beautifully captures the sacrifices made in the name of devotion, proving that true emotional connections can endure across time, distance, and adversity.

title: Journey Beyond the Himalayas
plot: Two Columbus (Journalist Prabesh Subedi and Artist Manoj Gajurel) crossed through the innumerable mountains, terraces and hillocks to reach the outlandish land and people of Mustang.

title: Madhesi
plot: Two mens' journeys to save south Nepal.

title: In Search of the Riyal
plot: A prospective of the Nepal immigrants leaving for the Gulf in order to earn some riyals (Iranian currency). With a quarter of the family members gone to work as cheap laborers, Nepal seems to have lost its self-sufficiency. From a family finance supported by money sent by a young son or husband from abroad to a society without any other means of income, there seems to be no way of knowing where the problem begins and ends. The only thing we can do is revisit the present situation of immigration, which is a result of a vicious cycle of individual danger, pain, agitation, fear, and repeated migration. It's not easy for the naive citizens of Nepal who grew up in a feudal agricultural society to delve into the capitalistic economy. What if the agency that introduced foreign employers is a fraud? What if their wage is delayed? What happens to a family without its head for years? What if the money from foreign lands is gone again? Not knowing what will happen next, the citizens of Nepal live another day chasing after the mirage of foreign laborers. Can they really find riyal?

title: Aastha
plot: A poignant and thought-provoking social drama that explores the profound weight of faith, devotion, and moral integrity (*Aastha*). Set within a close-knit community caught between traditional spiritual values and the harsh, corrupting pressures of modern life, the narrative follows a principled protagonist whose deeply held beliefs are pushed to their absolute limit by an unexpected family crisis and societal hypocrisy. Forced to navigate a complex ethical minefield, they must decide whether to compromise their core values for survival or hold fast to their convictions, capturing a powerful, emotionally charged struggle to protect what is truly sacred in a rapidly changing world.

title: The Sound of One Leg Dancing
plot: In a desperate search for happiness, a one-legged seventeen year old girl from a very poor home walks into a surreal TV stage to compete in a glamorous dance program. She believes winning will help her overcome a traumatic childhood in which her parents kept her hidden indoors and the society laughed at her for being a sinner in a previous life.

title: Bakshis
plot: A compelling drama that explores the intricate dynamics of power, privilege, and the complex nature of human generosity. Set within a community where social hierarchies and personal ambition intersect, the film delves into the moral dilemmas that arise when the lines between altruism and manipulation become blurred. As the narrative unfolds, the characters are forced to confront the true price of "gifts" received and given, ultimately revealing how deep-seated personal motivations and societal pressures can reshape relationships and test the very foundations of trust.

title: Mamaghar
plot: A woman raises her son with his uncle, when she is forced to leave her home after her mother-in-law's death.

title: Life on the Road Nepal
plot: A drive through some of the most beautiful sceneries of Nepal after the 2015 Earthquake.

title: Daughters of Everest
plot: DAUGHTERS OF EVEREST brings a unique flavor to the familiar story of the attempt to climb Mount Everest. In 2000, the first-ever expedition of Sherpa women to climb Everest was organized. This captivating documentary gives a close-up account of the expedition. To the Sherpa people, the ethnic group legendary for their unmatched skill in mountaineering, the mountain is a holy place. Mount Everest is "Chomolongma" - Mother Goddess of the Universe. Ironically, Sherpa women have long been discouraged from climbing the powerful and daunting Everest, relegated instead to more traditional roles as homemakers or into support roles in the prestigious climbing industry of Nepal. The documentary follows the five diverse, charismatic women chosen for this historic expedition. Told from a woman's perspective rarely seen on Everest or off, the film is a dramatic, inspiring Everest story and an absorbing portrait of the Sherpa community.

title: Baimani
plot: A gritty and intense social drama that delves into the dark underbelly of deception, greed, and moral compromise (*Baimani*). The narrative tracks the lives of ordinary individuals driven to extreme measures by desperate circumstances, highlighting how a single act of betrayal can trigger a destructive domino effect through a tight-knit community. As trust fractures and personal ambitions clash with ethical boundaries, the film presents a powerful, thought-provoking examination of the heavy price of dishonesty and the difficult path toward reclaiming one's integrity in a world fraught with temptation.

title: Talk of the town USA
plot: An engaging and fast-paced talk show that turns a spotlight on the unique culture, local personalities, and defining stories shaping communities across America. Through lively roundtable discussions, celebrity guest interviews, and deep dives into current lifestyle and entertainment trends, *Talk of the town USA* captures the vibrant pulse of contemporary American life, offering viewers an entertaining and insightful look at the topics everyone is discussing.

title: Denied: A Life Confined
plot: For many disabled and other marginalized populations, economic and educational barriers are significant and access to services and a life with dignity is a struggle. This documentary is a portrait of a severely disabled young man in Nepal, whose world is confined to one room but whose daily life is sustained by his family's love and care.

title: The Spirit Doesn't Come Anymore
plot: This moving short documentary profiles Pao Wangchuk, an elderly Tibetan shaman living in a refugee camp in Pokhara, Nepal. As the last in an unbroken thirteen-generation lineage of spiritual mediums, he faces a deep crisis when his eldest son resists inheriting the sacred craft. Through their intimate generational conflict, the film masterfully explores the painful loss of cultural heritage and the vulnerability of ancient traditions in a modern world.

title: Scaling the Mountain
plot: This insightful short documentary explores the vital intersection of community-led forest conservation and reproductive health in rural Nepal. The film highlights how localized environmental management initiatives empower indigenous communities and families to restore their surrounding natural resources. Through intimate interviews and personal stories, it showcases the profound impact that integrating healthcare with ecological preservation has on community resilience and long-term sustainability.

title: Yesarinai Aaru Phulchha
plot: The war between the Gov and the Maoists rebels ran for ten years in Nepal. In the film we are trying to include the feelings of a mother who has lost her son.

title: Festival of Tihar
plot: A spiritual journey of Nepal. Festivals of Tihar is a five-day Hindu festival reverence to the Gods, the bonding between animals and Humans; hence each day Crows, Dogs, Cows, Oxen and Brothers are worshipped.

title: Ke Bho Lau Na Ni
plot: This lighthearted romantic drama follows a vibrant cast of characters navigating the unpredictable, often humorous twists of modern love and familial expectations. When a series of comical misunderstandings threatens to derail a promising romance, the protagonists must rely on their wits and the support of friends to set things right. Ultimately, the film delivers an entertaining and heartwarming exploration of devotion, resilience, and the power of communication in bridging relationship gaps.

title: Samaya Ra Paristhiti
plot: How often do we come to terms with ourselves? How often do we find time to stand and stare for a while? Do we even breathe a sigh of relief? How often have we asked ourselves if we really are happy? Is what you are doing what you always wanted to do? Do you notice the humming of birds anymore? Do you notice the cool breeze of wind kissing your cheeks as you walk by? Do you feel the selfless emotions anymore? Do you ever feel this that you are not being yourself but somebody you are ought to be? Do you live between the moments or are they the moments that handcuff you the way they want you to live? Are you happy with the countless compromised you make with your soul everyday? Do you realize how the time is slipping by and you and I are dancing to the the tunes of circumstances? Samaya Ra Paristhiti is all about this.

title: Pijanda
plot: This action-packed romantic drama marks the iconic debut of Nikhil Upreti as an idealistic young man who stands up against rampant crime and corruption. When local underworld forces threaten the community and tear apart innocent lives, he is forced to use his unmatched physical skills to wage a one-man war for justice. Amidst high-octane action and deep personal sacrifices, the film captures his fierce struggle to break free from a metaphorical cage of societal lawlessness and protect the people he loves.

title: Eve Teasing
plot: This striking short film delivers a powerful and compact look at the pervasive reality of public street harassment faced by women daily. Through its brief but tense runtime, the narrative follows a young woman navigating her routine commute while confronting the unsettling attention of bystanders. Ultimately, the film serves as a sharp social commentary, urging viewers to recognize the psychological impact of harassment and the urgent need for safer public spaces.

title: Palla Ghar Ko Jhyal Ma
plot: This nostalgic short musical captures the bittersweet pining and innocent charm of a neighborhood romance. Through a series of poetic and rhythmic interactions, a young lover looks across to a neighboring window, hoping for a glimpse of their affection. The film uses vibrant musical sequences and traditional melodies to beautifully express the universal ache and joy of unspoken, youthful love.

title: Baacha Bandhan
plot: This emotionally charged family drama explores the sacred weight of a solemn promise made between loved ones across generations. When unforeseen hardships and conflicting personal ambitions threaten to tear a tight-knit family apart, the characters are forced to choose between their individual desires and their familial obligations. Ultimately, the film delivers a powerful message about the enduring power of devotion, sacrifice, and the unbreakable bonds forged by keeping one's word.

title: Rolpa Rog
plot: Set against the turbulent backdrop of the decade-long armed conflict in the Rolpa district of Nepal, this poignant social drama follows the lives of ordinary villagers caught in the crossfire of war. The narrative explores the heavy psychological and physical toll that the civil unrest inflicts on a tight-knit community struggling to maintain their daily livelihoods and relationships. Through powerful personal struggles and emotional sacrifices, the film captures a deeply moving portrait of survival, resilience, and the devastating "disease" of war that reshapes their homeland.

title: Riingata
plot: Set in Northern Nepal, during the climax of the civil war that consumed thousands of lives and caused an unprecedented loss and damage to property,

title: Nepal Talent Hunt
plot: This reality television series showcases a nationwide search to discover and celebrate the most exceptionally gifted individuals across Nepal. Aspiring performers from various backgrounds compete in front of a panel of judges, displaying diverse skills ranging from music and dance to unique variety acts. Through intense weekly eliminations and public voting, the show captures the emotional journeys of contestants as they vie for a life-changing grand prize and the ultimate title.

title: The Blessing
plot: In this high-stakes short action-adventure film, a daring protagonist embarks on a treacherous mission to secure a sacred and mysterious artifact known as "The Blessing." Navigating a landscape fraught with deadly traps, rugged terrain, and ruthless adversaries, they must rely on peak physical skill and quick thinking to survive. Ultimately, the pulse-pounding journey becomes a test of true resilience, exploring whether the ultimate prize is worth the immense sacrifices required to claim it.

title: Suruwaat: Euta Naya Jiwan
plot: This poignant drama captures the emotionally raw journey of individuals seeking a fresh start after hitting rock bottom due to substance abuse and societal isolation. Through a series of interconnected personal struggles, the characters confront their past traumas while navigating the painful and complex road to rehabilitation and recovery. Ultimately, the film serves as an inspiring narrative on resilience, highlighting the transformative power of forgiveness, self-worth, and the hope that comes with a new beginning.

title: Memory
plot: PRAKASH lonely unmarried boy visits different places to get rid off the memory .

title: Gaun Ramililo
plot: This charming short musical offers a joyful and nostalgic celebration of traditional rural life and the vibrant community spirit of a Nepalese village. Through lively, rhythmic melodies and colorful folk dances, the film captures the simple pleasures, seasonal festivities, and tight-knit bonds shared by the locals. Ultimately, it serves as a heartwarming visual love letter to the cultural heritage, warmth, and enduring beauty found within country life.

title: Bhatbhate
plot: This compelling short film centers on a middle-class protagonist navigating the crushing weight of economic struggle and societal expectations in urban Nepal. As the pressures of daily life mount, a humble motorbike—affectionately known as a "Bhatbhate"—becomes a critical lifeline and a poignant symbol of survival. Through its intimate 13-minute runtime, the narrative offers a raw and deeply human look at the relentless perseverance required to keep moving forward against all odds.

title: Moving Mountains
plot: Moving Mountains draws on experiences of Kathmandu's catastrophic 1934 quake to highlight the seismic threat to the city and explain what people can do to prepare.

title: Dui Kinara
plot: This poignant romantic drama follows two lovers who are deeply separated by rigid social class divides and conflicting family obligations. Like two parallel shores (*Dui Kinara*) that can never meet, they struggle to keep their love alive while facing relentless pressure from traditional societal expectations. Through a narrative filled with emotional sacrifice, the film presents a touching look at the resilience of the human heart when forced to choose between personal happiness and familial duty.

title: Nata Ragat Ko
plot: This emotionally powerful family drama explores the profound strength of familial ties, biological connections, and the deep moral obligations that bind people together. When an unexpected family crisis forces the characters to confront long-held secrets and painful personal truths, they are pushed to the brink of emotional collapse. Through themes of deep sacrifice, forgiveness, and unconditional love, the film highlights how the "bond of blood" (*Nata Ragat Ko*) ultimately triumphs over societal pressures and external hardships to heal a fractured family.

title: The Lost Locket
plot: Each night a girl wakes up to a mysterious dream. When she goes on a journey find the answers for her dreams, she finds out it had been a trap set for her.

title: The Last Evening on Earth
plot: A man who is back from space visits his grand daughter before dying.

title: Sannani
plot: This classic Nepalese drama delves into the emotional complexities of traditional family dynamics, societal expectations, and personal sacrifice. The narrative follows a resilient female protagonist, Sannani, as she navigates the trials of rural life, rigid social structures, and the shifting responsibilities of love and duty. Through her personal joys and hardships, the film offers a poignant and reflective look at the strength of the human spirit and the enduring power of family bonds in a changing world.

title: Tapasya
plot: This emotionally gripping drama captures a profound story of intense personal sacrifice, devotion, and structural family duty. The narrative centers on a resilient protagonist who willingly paths a difficult, self-sacrificing road—a metaphorical penance (*Tapasya*)—to ensure the happiness, education, and well-being of their loved ones. Confronting harsh societal judgments, economic hardships, and deep personal loneliness, the film delivers a powerful and classic exploration of unconditional love and the immense weight of familial responsibility.

title: Gyalmu's House
plot: A woman of great courage, humour and dignity rebuilds her life high in the mountains after an earthquake of terrible power devastated Nepal's Langtang Valley.

title: Rajamati
plot: Add a PlotDirected by Neer Shah, *Rajamati* (1995) is a landmark Nepal Bhasa film based on a classic Newari folk ballad. It tells the tragic story of a beautiful Kathmandu woman whose life and romance are shattered by societal pressures and an unhappy arranged marriage.

title: Hiccups
plot: Sarala must face herself in the mirror when she falls in love with a thief.

title: The Great Mukhiya
plot: An uptight head of a village has to hunt down a ferocious wild boar to pose for the first ever photograph in his tyrannical lineage.

title: Idiosyncratic
