plot: A man with an absurd behavior tries to overcome his past. His desire to move on is trapped despite his efforts. Relentlessly, he accepts that his existence in this world is worthless.

title: Sex, Partying & Peer Pressure
plot: Directed by Keith Allmon, *Sex, Partying & Peer Pressure* (2015) is a two-minute dramatic short. It delivers a fast-paced warning to teenagers about the rapid, dangerous consequences of giving in to negative social dynamics and substance abuse at youth parties.

title: Gold in the Ashes
plot: A Nepalese hospice doctor at a Hindu, open-air cremation site in Kathmandu, struggles to make sense of life and death in the moments surrounding the devastating 2015 earthquake.

title: Child Centre Out of School Program
plot: Directed by Sudip Shrestha, *Child Centre Out of School Program* (2005) is a 13-minute Nepali documentary short. It highlights grassroots educational initiatives designed to provide non-formal learning opportunities, support, and resources to marginalized children lacking access to the formal school system.

title: Chungi (Colours Of Life)
plot: Various colours involved in daily life portrayed through a child's play.

title: Purpose of Living
plot: A Story about a girl who is depressed about her daily life and surrounding which leads her to think suicide is the only key for freedom. Can she overcome her depression or will it end her precious life.

title: Khandan
plot: Sudha, who is pregnant, never thought that Gyatri Subedi, her mother-in-law, would hatch such a cruel conspiracy against her just after her newlywed husband Vijaya left for the city. Subsequently, Gyatri, with the help of Manthara, bribes a witch-doctor to declare Sudha a witch.

title: Nilo Aakash
plot: *Nilo Aakash* (2006) is a Nepali musical short film. It utilizes a rhythmic, song-driven narrative structure to capture personal reflections, emotional expression, and cultural themes against the backdrop of contemporary daily life.

title: Bhagya Le Jurayo
plot: Directed by Resh Raj Acharya, *Bhagya Le Jurayo* (2002) is a Nepali feature film starring Dhiren Shakya, Sanchita Luitel, and Rejina Uprety, with a special appearance by Rajesh Hamal. The social drama revolves around themes of destiny, love, and family dynamics, exploring how twist of fate (*Bhagya*) shapes the characters' relationships and life choices.

title: Euphues
plot: A suicide bomber on his mission tries to confront his partner who faces dilemmas about the rightness of their doing.

title: Punte Parade
plot: Directed by Subash Koirala, *Punte Parade* (2014) is a Nepali comedy-drama and romance starring Samyam Puri, Anjila Dahal, and Nilu Doma Sherpa, featuring a special appearance by Priyanka Karki. The film captures the humorous, chaotic, and emotional ups and downs of youth, tracking a group of young friends as they navigate the trials of growing up, changing relationships, and first love.

title: Fulai Fulko Mausam Timilai
plot: Romance between two people which ends up in disaster but end up together at the end.

title: Supportive Men
plot: A village in Southern Nepal shows how gender equality can be achieved when men change their behavior and help with housework.

title: Alpaviram
plot: Directed by Narayan Puri, *Alpaviram* (2006) is a Nepali social drama starring Rajesh Hamal and Vijay Lama. The film delves into the heavy emotional tolls of life's transitions, exploring themes of pause, reflection, and the complex choices individuals face when their personal and professional lives reach a critical turning point.

title: Bhor
plot: Directed by Yadav Kumar Bhattarai and written by Krishna Dharabasi, *Bhor* (2018) is a poignant Nepali social drama starring Ramanarayan Thakur, Anitarani Mandal, and Parmesh Jha. The film deeply explores the devastating socio-economic and psychological impacts of the dowry system within society, highlighting a family's struggles as they look for the "first light" (*Bhor*) of justice and social change.

title: Heavenly Place Manang
plot: A traveler travels to Manang and explores the wildlife, Throngla pass, lifestyle, ancient monasteries, re- incarnation of a monk after 600 years, different festivals practiced through out the year.

title: Sparsa
plot: Directed by Min Bahadur Bham, *Sparsa* (2012) is a Nepali short drama. The film relies on subtle, visual storytelling to explore themes of human connection, emotional vulnerability, and the unseen social dynamics that shape interpersonal relationships in contemporary Nepal.

title: Patachara
plot: Directed by Ramkrishna Khadgi, *Patachara* (2009) is a significant Nepal Bhasa film starring Melina Manandhar and Karma Shakya. Based on the poignant Buddhist Jataka tale, it follows the tragic journey of a wealthy merchant's daughter who endures unimaginable personal losses before finding solace, redemption, and spiritual enlightenment through the teachings of Gautama Buddha.

title: Silver Bangles
plot: The film is based on unusual relation between a Dalit boy and a Brahamin girl, their struggle to succeed for a new lease of life and post union effort for a baby in the time when the entire community was under the threat from armed insurgency.

title: Kaidi
plot: Produced by Sandesh Movies, *Kaidi* (2001) is a Nepali action-drama starring prominent actor Dhiren Shakya. The film centers on themes of crime, justice, and redemption, following the struggles of a prisoner (*Kaidi*) navigating the harsh realities of the legal system and societal prejudice while trying to clear his name.

title: Ko Hola Tyo
plot: Directed by Satiz Shrestha, *Ko Hola Tyo* (2012) is a highly popular Nepali musical short and music video that serves as the title track for singer Sunil Giri's debut album. Starring Paul Shah and Prakriti Shrestha in their career-defining on-screen debuts, the visual narrative follows a sweet, relatable story of youthful attraction and romance, tracking a young man captivated by a mysterious woman and wondering who she might be (*Ko Hola Tyo*).

title: Crow in the Mist
plot: An old fisherman overcomes his solitude by playing a game with his own reflection as a child in a river bank.

title: Disenchanted Cinema
plot: The film Disenchanted Cinema takes us on a short journey to three remote mountainous villages in Mid-Western Nepal. In these villages, German anthropologist Michael Oppitz shot his legendary ethnographic film Shamans of the Blind Country (1981). A mobile cinema brings the film back to the places where it was shot and gives the new generations an opportunity to revisit their own history through the ''magic'' of cinema. Viewing their own past and present everyday life on the screen, the spectators go through a disenchanting experience.

title: Bihani Ko Mirmere
plot: *Bihani Ko Mirmere* (2003) is a Nepali musical short film. The narrative weaves music and visual storytelling to capture the emotional essence of a fresh dawn (*Bihani Ko Mirmere*), focusing on themes of personal renewal, hope, and reflective human connections.

title: Misheal pariyar
plot: documentary - about my real life

title: Aavas
plot: Directed by Surendra Poudel, *Aavas* (2015) is a Nepali romantic musical starring Samyam Puri, Ashma D.C., Salon Basnet, and Nisha Adhikari. The film follows a youth-centric storyline exploring contemporary love, friendship, and personal aspirations, driven by a highly popular soundtrack.

title: In the Shadow of Darjeeling
plot: Tells the story of Nepal's modern tea industry and its struggle to define itself on the world stage against the backdrop of its more famous, colonially-nurtured neighbor, Darjeeling. The film follows leading tea producers in Kathmandu as they journey to Hong Kong to showcase their teas along with hundreds of other Asian tea producers at the Hong Kong International Tea Expo. What emerges is a rich story about humble entrepreneurs struggling to crack an international industry - which is symbolic of Nepal's larger struggle to grow economically and compete globally.

title: Batuli Ko Chura
plot: Produced by Abhinash Bikram Shah and directed by Anup Poudel, *Batuli Ko Chura* (2013) is a poignant 3-minute Nepali short drama starring Prasun Ghimire and Riya Tamsang. The film follows Batuli, an 8-year-old girl fascinated by grown-up beauty products—especially colorful glass bangles (*chura*). When a street vendor arrives in her village offering bangles in exchange for old plastic goods, Batuli is so desperate to join her friends that she contemplates trading her only pair of new sandals, telling a sweet, innocent story about childhood desires and the lengths to which children will go to fulfill their dreams.

title: Aaroha: Rising Together
plot: "Equality is also respecting the worth of a life, may it be a man or a woman" I am a person first ......everything else comes later "I could be a she or a he But I could definitely be Me" Respect me for what I am and not who I am I don't want what others have. I don't want more of what others have. I just want what is mine and how much I deserve. It is that simple Women Empowerment is not a start. It should be an outcome of Gender equality.

title: Shree 5 Ambare
plot: Ambar (Saugat Malla) and Kali (Keki Adhikari) meet and fell in love in their childhood on street. Ambar is left along in the street when Kali is taken away by a guy. She became a prostitute and lived a comfortable life while Ambar nourished his desire to become a king in the society.

title: Peaks of Emotion: The Children of Ocean Nepal
plot: This short documentary will tell the story of four teenager girls who study at the American Community School in Abu Dhabi, UAE. They have been serving at a Nepalese orphanage for a week every year.

title: Chakre
plot: *Chakre* is a Nepali action-thriller feature film that features actress Afsana Thapa in the role of Maya. The film dives deep into the high-stakes underworld of local crime syndicates and gang culture, tracking the intense conflicts, power struggles, and cycle of vengeance that unravel when rival factions collide on the streets.

title: Pirati Aafai Hudon Rahechha
plot: Directed by Narayan Puri, *Pirati Aafai Hudon Rahechha* (2001) is a Nepali romantic drama film starring Dhiren Shakya, Melina Manandhar, and Ramesh Upreti. The film revolves around the complexities of human relationships, exploring how love (*Pirati*) often blossoms naturally and unexpectedly, while navigating the emotional challenges and societal expectations that come with it.

title: Ragat
plot: The social drama explores complex human relationships, personal sacrifice, and the unbreakable ties of blood (Ragat), tracking the emotional and moral conflicts that unfold when familial duties and societal challenges collide.

title: Young Torture: Ek Phera Hasana
plot: Directed by Nitin Chand, *Young Torture: Ek Phera Hasana* (2014) is a Nepali musical short film. The 4-minute visual narrative captures the upbeat and dramatic themes of youthful energy, tracking the emotional dynamics and vibrant romance between young lovers.

title: The Price of Childhood
plot: The story of working children in Nepal is a complicated one. It is a story of traditional worlds destroyed by modern forces, of ethnic oppression, of political upheaval, of simple cruelty and remarkable hope. The Price of Childhood seeks to explain this phenomenon through the narratives of those who live with child labor: children, parents, owners, activists, government, scholars, and normal folks we meet along the way. In better understanding the situation from these various perspectives, the film aims to assist in improving the lives of those who suffer.

title: Mani, the Hidden Valley of Happiness at a Crossroads
plot: A first time portrait of the ancient monuments (Mani) in Tsum, the Beyul Kyimolung/Hidden Valley of Happiness Pilgrimage area, in the high Himalayas of Nepal, and the opinions of the Tsum people on the up-coming international motorway development aiming to connect Tibet Autonomous Region of China and India that is a potential threat to the thousand year old monuments, cultural heritage sites and intact traditions.

title: Dukha
plot: Directed by Deepak Shrestha, *Dukha* (2003) is a Nepali social drama starring Rajesh Hamal, Niruta Singh, Dilip Rayamajhi, and Sunil Thapa. The film explores the profound hardships, societal obstacles, and emotional struggles endured by its characters, capturing the true essence of its title (*Dukha*/Suffering) through themes of family conflict and personal sacrifice.

title: The Hermit's Tea Party
plot: Two Sadhu's, a guru and his disciple, live together on the grounds of the Pashupatinath Temple. The Hermit's Tea Party is a glimpse into their daily routines of their spiritual life, and what they have left behind.

title: Lekhirahada
plot: Directed by Baburam Raut, *Lekhirahada* (2014) is a Nepali short film starring Apurva Rai, Rajesh Hamal, and Subigya Sunder Shrestha. Produced by Infuse Pictures, the brief narrative delves into reflective themes of human life, choices, and destiny, capturing the deep emotional conflicts and changing perspectives of an individual contemplating their future.

title: The Making of Himalaya DVD Extra
plot: Directed by Eric Valli, *The Making of Himalaya* (2000) is a short documentary feature that provides a behind-the-scenes look at the production of the Oscar-nominated film *Himalaya*. It captures the logistical challenges, cinematography, and authentic cultural collaboration involved in bringing the epic story of remote mountain life to the screen.

title: Ma Yesto Geet Gauchu
plot: Directed by Sudarshan Thapa, *Ma Yesto Geet Gauchu* (2017) is a commercially successful Nepali romantic musical drama starring Paul Shah and Pooja Sharma. Driven by a highly popular soundtrack, the film explores a youth-centric storyline centered on modern romance, emotional misunderstandings, and the heavy sacrifices young lovers make for each other's happiness.

title: Mustang Secrets Beyond the Himalayas
plot: A traveler sets on a journey into the remote mountains of Nepal, to explore Annapurna Region; and learns about the incredibly unique culture, festivals and spiritual traditions of the locals.

title: Chokhaunee
plot: It is a tale of indigenous woman, who lacks compassion and care from her husband- leads her to sleep with another guy.She is punished in the name of custom while her husband's negligence and evil eye of another man is culprit.

title: Khaacho
plot: "Khaacho" is a nepali word defining about the NEED. Need small thing in our daily life makes us great changes.A story of a carpenter helping people just with a Nail and giving happiness and...                See full synopsis »

title: Quality Education Resource
plot: *Quality Education Resource* (2005) is a short documentary film that examines educational development and training initiatives. Spanning 13 minutes, the documentary focuses on the distribution, implementation, and overall impact of modern learning tools and resources designed to enhance teaching environments and improve student learning outcomes.

title: Sa Karnali
plot: Thinley and Amrit are traders travelling on business. The much older Thinley hands Amrit a 'Company Mala' a necklace of silver coins from colonial India and another lined with the tusks of a musk deer so that the young man may get himself a bride.

title: Duniya
plot: Directed, written, and produced by Shiva Regmi, *Duniya* (2006) is an acclaimed Nepali social drama starring Bhuwan K.C., Nikhil Upreti, Rekha Thapa, and Niruta Singh. The film explores profound social issues, shifting relationships, and human struggles, capturing poignant family dynamics and societal themes that earned Regmi the National Film Award for Best Director.

title: Champion
plot: *Champion* (2016) is an Indian Telugu-language television quiz game show hosted by Baladitya. The competitive series features high school students from across Telangana and Andhra Pradesh testing their academic knowledge, quick thinking, and general awareness across multiple rounds to see who will be crowned the ultimate champion.

title: Kokh
plot: Unmarried sex-worker is suffering from extreme dilemma when she gets redundant pregnancy whether to conceive a baby or not. She fights with her thoughts.

title: Premalaya
plot: Directed by Kumar Shah, *Premalaya* is a Nepali romantic drama film starring Nirajan Pradhan, Mariska Pokharel, Sunny Dhakal, Surya Thapa, and Rabindra Jha. The film delves into a modern, youth-centric narrative exploring the deep emotional complexities of contemporary love, relationship dynamics, and individual life choices.

title: Daughters' Destiny
plot: Nepalese women are telling their stories arranged marriages. In the jungle of western Nepal lives Fofo, who is married off at the age of 10 to a man of 20. When Thuli becomes a widow after 10 childbirths she is mocked at by both neighbours and relatives. Savitri did not even know the name of the groom she was marrying and she was not even allowed to see him during the wedding ceremony. Nepalese law does not allow child marriage but the law does not reach girls who have never been to school and are living in areas with no roads. This film shows some of the reasons behind the 40 % guerrilla women in the Nepalese Maoist army.

title: Parivartan
plot: Directed by Hira Singh Khatri and adapted from a drama by Janardhan Sama, *Parivartan* (1971) is a classic Nepali social drama starring Yagya Nath Ghimire, Rita Thapa, and Nir Shah. The film focuses on themes of social transformation and community reform in rural Nepal, highlighting state initiatives like the "Back to the Village Campaign" through the emotional tension between traditional practices and modern societal progress.

title: The Solitude
plot: Writer James Woolf tries to find the perfect ending for the novel "Solitude" until he realizes his own infinite solitude and the connection of his novel with Virginia Woolf's "A Room of One's Own."

title: Sahid Gate
plot: The film explores poignant themes of patriotism, civic duty, and sacrifice, highlighting personal and familial struggles that intersect with societal ideals and the deeper, symbolic legacy of the nation's martyrs.

title: Strike
plot: Father with an emergency to reach hospital with blood for the child comes across with strike happening in the town and its consequence.

title: Shambhala
plot: A young bride in Nepal must face the realities of marriage when her husband returns home from working abroad.

title: Yastai Rahecha Jindagi
plot: Directed by Bishwa Basnet with music by Shambhujeet Baskota, *Yastai Rahecha Jindagi* (2002) is a Nepali drama film starring Arunima Lamsal. The movie explores the unpredictability of life, tracing the emotional hardships, struggles, and changing fortunes of its characters as they navigate personal and societal challenges.

title: Akinchn
plot: The movie, beautifully created on the coordination of socio-political reality, has made the main subject of the defective burning social issues like poverty, human discrimination, and child labour and exploitation prevailing in the world. Various politicians have been pushing the youth into wars and conflicts attracting them just for their personal prestige for ages; they have been ruling over the weak ones with consolation of liberty and sovereignty; the movie comes into a conclusion that the entire administrative system of the world has been unsuccessful to establish a society free of poverty, inequality and exploitation carrying a load of weapons on the back and delivering sweet lectures for peace.

title: Foundation for Hope: Year One of Relief, Recovery and Reconstruction in Nepal
plot: The documentary showcases the personal stories, in their own words, of a few of the millions of Nepalis that were affected by the earthquake of April 2015 and the feats of extraordinary courage, grace, and resilience of the Nepali people as a whole.

title: Hotel Elam
plot: Tall pine trees, ups and downs of hills, snake like curve of roads and a small tin-thatched hotel at a corner, welcomes any passer by. The middle-aged owner is a lonely dignified lady who is strong as pine trees and knows her business well keeping all curious eyes at a distance. A soft speaking gentle bank manager covers almost fifty kilometers from planes to hills daily. A nature lover, workaholic, the long distance offers him a rescue from his mundane job and loneliness. One day both the characters face each other in a traumatic situation where humanity wins but the fatal accident actually changes both lives leaving the lady in an illusive reality.

title: Eviction
plot: Directed by Grady Walker, *Eviction* (2006) is a short documentary film that sheds light on a historic humanitarian crisis. The 10-minute narrative focuses on the mass eviction of ethnic Nepalis from the Kingdom of Bhutan in the early 1990s, capturing the ongoing struggles and personal experiences of the tens of thousands of refugees living in camps across eastern Nepal.

title: Rog
plot: Prateek is 20, but he will be soon dying because of AIDS. When he's running away from everything, he suddenly bumps into Shristi, a young photographer who is in love with him. It's up to Prateek to choose between love over death or vice versa.

title: BabuSaheb
plot: Directed by Ramesh Budhathoki, *Babu Saheb* (2002) is a Nepali social drama film starring Bhuwan K.C., Karishma Manandhar, Ramesh Upreti, and Pooja Chand. The film centers on themes of familial duty, social status, and personal relationships, exploring the emotional conflicts and sacrifices that arise within a traditional family setting navigating modern societal expectations.

title: Namari Baanchey
plot: Marking a nostalgic reunion of The Original Duo (Phiroj Shyangden and Adrian Pradhan), this melodic piece reflects on time, survival, and the enduring nature of human relationships if one lives long enough.

title: Ghar Desh
plot: The film captures the internal conflict and emotional journey of an immigrant torn between the stability of life abroad and a deep-seated pull to return to their native roots.

title: Dependent Life
plot: Directed by Aashish Limbu and featuring members of the Mainali family, this intimate documentary explores the lived realities, systemic obstacles, and everyday resilience of individuals navigating life with severe physical dependencies.

title: Kathmandu Nepal Durbar
plot: Clocking in at three minutes, this brief visual dispatch chronicles the tragic devastation and immediate community response at the UNESCO World Heritage site of Kathmandu Durbar Square following the catastrophic April 2015 earthquake.

title: Cheli Beti
plot: The story of Nepali women sold into prostitution in Indian cities and the challenges they face in reintegrating back into society upon returning home.

title: Shadhyantra
plot: The film chronicles a web of political conspiracies and deep-seated betrayals as rival underground factions plot against each other for institutional control and power.

title: Bibash
plot: Meaning "Helpless," this poignant family short explores the heavy emotional toll and tough choices a desperate individual faces while trying to keep their household afloat through a sudden financial crisis.

title: Chha Ekan Chha
plot: A girl is looking for a boy to get married. In course of time, she dates six boys. Who will she choose among the six?

title: Rage: Aakrosh
plot: AAKROSH (RAGE) follows the story of a single woman struggling to seek justice from the authorities for the road expansion. The Movie questions the state's existing laws and regulations regarding property rights.

title: The Path to Fool's Paradise
plot: Directed by Kiran Thapa and starring Nirmala Sharma and Sushmita Karki, this brief romantic drama explores the emotional traps, unrealistic expectations, and psychological disillusionment that surface when individuals chase a superficial or flawed idea of love.

title: Kagaj
plot: When one really wants to do something its not IMPOSSIBLE. A compelling story of how a small action can lead to bigger changes in life of not one but the lives of many. If one really know the value and importance of education, one can find it anywhere. Even in trash. A single soul carrying the hopes of many can educate the world.

title: Hope Remains
plot: Lasting just a single minute, this hyper-short visual study captures a fleeting moment of deep isolation or quiet resilience, offering a poetic reflection on maintaining inner faith during dark personal times.

title: Durgamandu
plot: Directed by Madhab Raj Kharel and adapted from his own novel, this feature exposes and challenges toxic superstitions and deeply entrenched systems—such as Chhaupadi (menstrual exile) and the persecution of single women as witches—in the remote, isolated communities of Far-Western Nepal.

title: Prem Apradha
plot: Love is a crime in a remote cultural bound tribe of Nepal. As of if some one did commit it their parents have to pay for it.

title: Janayuddha
plot: Directed by Bimal Poudel with an ensemble cast including Sushil Chhetri and Nandita K.C., this gritty historical feature dives straight into the psychological trauma, civilian casualties, and heavy emotional weight of the decade-long Nepalese Maoist People's War (Janayuddha).

title: Jun Hau Timi Phool Hau Timi
plot: Centered around a poetic, song-driven narrative framework, this romantic musical short utilizes lyrical imagery to chronicle the escalating courtship and deep emotional vows exchanged between a young couple.

title: Secreat tree: The Birth of Buddha
plot: This Documentry film is whole life histry of Lord Buddha.

title: Dulahi
plot: Directed by Baburam Dhakal and starring Rajesh Hamal, this domestic drama centers on the emotional challenges, societal pressures, and shifting household relationships experienced by a newlywed bride as she adapts to her husband's home.

title: Khel
plot: This brief documentary explores grassroots athletic development in regional communities, highlighting how access to structured physical play empowers underprivileged and tribal youth.

title: Anjuli
plot: Starring Sushil Chhetri and Jharana Bajracharya, this late-classic feature follows a story of deep emotional devotion, framing the trials and tribulations a young couple faces when family pride threatens their union.

title: Love U Man
plot: A unique love story film in Nepali cinema history.

title: Mahadevi
plot: : Featuring an all-star vintage cast including Bhuwan KC, Karishma Manandhar, and Sunil Thapa, this high-stakes narrative blends devotional motifs with classic action as virtue battles tyrannical, corrupt village forces.

title: Aago 2
plot: Serving as a standalone sequel to the iconic 2000 political film Aago, this thriller stars Sushil Chhetri and digs into a cyclical narrative of systemic corruption, student union power struggles, and underground political unrest.

title: Kumva Karan
plot: Featuring Bhola Raj Sapkota, Gaurav Pahari, and Nisha Adhikari, this lighthearted feature uses a blend of local humor and situational irony to track the comedic chaos that ensues when eccentric, sluggish villagers get mixed up in unforeseen local dilemmas.

title: Naya Jhul
plot: A story of a rebel; Ritu, Passionately in love with Sanjaya, until an incident that swerves her mode of life in a different direction, totally changing her attitude and perspective towards Life, Love, and Relationship.

title: The Moon
plot: What can you buy? What can you sell?

title: Khushi
plot: A young boy runs away from home because he failed his exams.

title: Omitted Project
plot: A lady whose occupation is midwifery but she doesn't have her own baby . at the end of her life change another direction.

title: Dui Duna Pannch
plot: Directed by and starring Jai Kishan Basnet alongside Karan Shrestha, this lighthearted commercial comedy follows a series of chaotic misunderstandings and wacky misadventures triggered by ordinary people getting caught up in absurd situations.

title: Children as Partners in Girl's Education
plot: This short documentary highlights grassroots advocacy in developing communities, showing how young children are actively mobilized as peer partners to promote gender equality and encourage the enrollment of young girls in school.

title: Life is Just a File
plot: Directed by Dikesh Khadgi Shahi, this surrealist animated short uses striking visual metaphors to critique the monotonous, bureaucratic nature of modern existence where human lives and identities are reduced to mere digital or paper files.

title: Bigu
plot: Nestled in the mountains of Dolakha, this observational short documentary captures the isolated spiritual lifestyles, ancient rituals, and daily endurance of the Buddhist nuns living at the remote Tashi Chime Gatsal Nunnery.

title: A Drop of Hope
plot: This localized short narrative highlights the severe water scarcity issues plaguing impoverished communities, following a family's daily emotional and physical struggles to secure clean water.

title: La Sorry
plot: Blending high-energy stunts with localized crime-thriller tropes, this film charts a relentless protagonist’s mission to seek street justice and protect his neighborhood from ruthless underground gangs.

title: Schools in the Crossfire
plot: This hard-hitting documentary examines the devastating impact of the Nepalese Civil War on the education system, detailing how innocent students and teachers were systematically targeted, abducted, and caught between Maoist rebels and government forces.

title: Birano Mayo
plot: Directed by Madhav Kharel and starring Namrata Sapkota, Rohit Rumba, and Dinesh DC, this emotionally charged feature chronicles the complex betrayals, sacrifices, and social hurdles that test the boundaries of a fragile love story.

title: Hearts in the Himalayas
plot: Of the 30,000 humanitarian organizations operating in Nepal, there is one that stands above the rest. From award-winning humanitarian journalist Debi Lang comes Hearts In The Himalayas, a documentary about the extraordinary efforts of Himalayan HealthCare. Only the most dedicated volunteers endure treacherous conditions and rugged terrain that take them into the Ganesh Himal region of the Himalayan mountain range in order to bring much needed care to the remote villages this Nepalese non-governmental agency serves. Co-founder Anil Parajuli leads an international medical team on an Everest-size expedition through the stunning beauty of the foothills of the Himalayas which mask the harsh reality of daily life for the villagers. It is a humanitarian adventure you soon won't forget.

title: If Men Had Periods: Purushpad
plot: Every woman and girl in Nepal deserves the right to manage their period in a safe and dignified way. Support our #PeriodPower campaign and tell the Nepal Government what you want to see in the new menstrual hygiene action plan.

title: The Faaadi Guys Show
plot: The incredible series of Comedy Musical Videos Featuring BiV1, Where new funny, hilarious ideas are visualized in every episodes - now running to web.

title: Nepal Music Festival 2014
plot: This special broadcast captures live performances, behind-the-scenes moments, and exclusive artist interviews from Nepal's premier volunteer-run music festival, showcasing the country's vibrant independent music scene.

title: Untitled Buddha Film in Nepal
plot: Nothing is permanent, not even for the enlightened one.

title: Mission Rebuild Nepal Stories
plot: This short documentary chronicles the devastating aftermath of the 2015 Nepal earthquake, highlighting the resilience of local communities and the grassroots relief efforts organized to rebuild shattered lives and infrastructure.

title: Queen of the Himalayas
plot: A pleasant and fascinating journey of lonely, Heartbroken British woman to the Himalayas ends into kill or get killed war.

title: Adhyaya
plot: Adhyaya weaves together four contrasting urban stories of love, betrayal, frustration, and psychological struggle within the modern, fast-paced life of Kathmandu.

title: Asamajhdari
plot: When a young couple meet for the first time before their wedding day they share their hopes and aspirations for their upcoming marriage and promise to support each other's dreams come what may. However, their life together takes an unexpected turn as the all too common reality of community expectations, peer pressure and family pressures set in. How should they respond? We first see the couple listening to those around them rather than to each other. They argue more and the distance and suspicions between them grow, until the shocking climax of the film shows just how wrong things can go between a husband and a wife. Take two, the film rewinds and this time the couple stay united despite the same pressures from friends and family. This time there is no shocking climax, at each juncture the couple try to talk and listen to each other and as a result are happier and their marriage is stronger. By showing how two approaches towards the daily pressures faced by a married couple can have such dramatically different consequences, the film asks audiences to reflect on their role in the success of their own marriage. Can happiness & mutual understanding between husband and wife rest in our own hands?

title: The Message
plot: A story of a British Soldier from the war zone where he expresses the pain, the emotions, the love etc etc, but something happens.

title: Refuse
plot: REFUSE is a word that refers not only to what we discard, but to a defiant act that can catalyze change. This video blends experimental and documentary techniques to reshape our understanding of the landscape and labor of trash-pickers. The layered, cinematic richness of the video belies its disturbing content and serves to underscore the indomitable human spirit.

title: Seams
plot: An existential journey into the fleeting moments of life which arise when volunteers in Nepal take a break from their work in order to celebrate. A spectrum of individuals, and the connections between them, guide the viewer through the trials and tribulations of communication and emotion.

title: Mama Bhanja
plot: Centered around the culturally rich and humorous dynamic between a maternal uncle (Mama) and his nephew (Bhanja), the story follows their playful schemes and shared family misadventures in a traditional setting.

title: Santan Ko Maya
plot: Starring Rajesh Hamal and Puja Chand, this classic feature explores the immense emotional sacrifices, hardships, and unconditional love parents endure to protect their children from societal hardships.

title: Best Friends Forever
plot: The narrative follows a close-knit group of young urban friends as their loyalty, romance, and shared dreams are put to the ultimate test by a series of unexpected personal betrayals

title: Ganesh Senchuri: Fanfani
plot: Driven by its rhythmic folk beat, this musical short visually frames a lively, lighthearted courtship dance between village youths flirting in a picturesque rural landscape.

title: Bihani
plot: Meaning "Morning," this early-2000s feature serves as a metaphor for fresh beginnings, tracking a struggling rural family's fight to overcome social injustice and greet a new dawn of financial stability.

title: Swarga Yahan
plot: Spanning four minutes, this lyrical presentation uses poetic imagery and spiritual melodies to illustrate how inner peace, family harmony, and the beauty of nature can turn any ordinary home into a literal heaven on Earth.

title: Secrets of Dhaulagiri
plot: This is a widely appreciated documentary form Nepal, already presented in several international film festivals. It explores the life obscured by innumerable ups and downs while living in the high Himalayas.

title: Adolescent Sexual and Reproductive Health
plot: This informational short documentary targets youth in developing regions to break down taboos, promote safe medical practices, and address critical awareness gaps regarding sexual health and contraceptive education.

title: Malati
plot: Centered on themes of female endurance and rural hardships, the narrative chronicles a woman's emotional and social battle to protect her household against traditional biases and economic adversity in a conservative village.

title: Acceptance
plot: Starring Resh Marhatta, this film-festival short delves into personal identity and human connection, exploring the emotional hurdles an individual must overcome to find validation and inner peace amidst societal judgment.

title: We Corner People
plot: Children, adults, and animals of a remote village in Nepal that has no electricity or shops, and is cut off from everything by a river, pride themselves on being "corner people."

title: Rupees 500
plot: Birkha Bahadur is local porter in Kathmandu Nepal who has many dreams like others. One day he makes Rs 500 after work, after that day he wants to change his life style and plans different things but something is waiting in his fate.....

title: Woolenmary
plot: Developed under Durgish Films, this unreleased project explores domestic struggles and emotional ties within a traditional household structure.

title: Sauta
plot: Serving as the second directorial feature from Narayan Puri, the film tackles the complex, often bitter domestic rivalries and social stigmas surrounding polygamy in Nepali society.

title: I am Always There For you
plot: Spanning just four minutes, this intimate short film centers on emotional reassurance and unconditional support between two individuals navigating a sudden personal crisis.

title: Maya Preeti
plot: Featuring veteran actor Jayanendra Chand Thakuri, this classic late-80s feature chronicles the intense social challenges and emotional sacrifices faced by a young couple fighting to keep their love alive.

title: Ghar
plot: In 2015, Nepal was devastated by two earthquakes. Seven high school students document the building of a new home and the spirit of the Sherpa people.

title: Virtual Dream
plot: In our lives, we are busy, tired, and sad - we perceive things from only one side - but what we see might not always be there.

title: Jeevan Yo Rahe Samma
plot: The short musical piece captures the enduring depth of love and partnership through a poignant, song-driven narrative that tests a couple's promise to stay together as long as life remains.

title: Mother
plot: The short musical piece captures the enduring depth of love and partnership through a poignant, song-driven narrative that tests a couple's promise to stay together as long as life remains.

title: Prithvi
plot: Rooted in classic 90s storytelling, this narrative balances family honor and systemic injustice as an upright protagonist fights against corrupt local factions to protect his community and loved ones.

title: Rock Band
plot: The film tracks the chaotic, high-stakes journey of a group of ambitious young musicians as they navigate interpersonal friction, industry exploitation, and personal sacrifices to make it big in the music scene.

title: Punte ko Pangro
plot: Punte ko Pangro is a story about a child in the countryside of the Kathmandu valley. We follow Punte, a playful boy, who has a toy wheel of his own, but he will do anything to get a bigger one. In his pursuit, he discovers his aspiration and ultimately own potential. The wheel is used as a symbol of supremacy to portray human desire and our passionate struggle to reach higher positions in society.

title: The Milk Man
plot: Kedar Phuyal's entire house collapsed during the the earthquake in Nepal on April 25th, 2015. He struggles to rebuild his life.

title: Naso
plot: Disillusioned by love, a woman seeks solace in a monastery in this modern drama.

title: Comma: Alpabiram
plot: Winner of "Project Hope Internation Film Festival" made with the theme- "Knowledge for Life", promoting traditional, indigenous and local knowledge and practices, to complement scientific knowledge in disaster risk management.

title: Pokhara.com
plot: weekly travel-based musical series takes viewers on a scenic and cultural journey through the breathtaking landscapes and folk music traditions of the Pokhara region.

title: Herne Katha
plot: Zubaida Khatun, like most of her friends got married at the age of 14 when she was studying at grade 7 . Zubaida's struggling journey from a girl married at early age to an aspiring politician in her area.

title: Pooja, Sir
plot: When two boys are kidnapped in a border town in Nepal, Detective Inspector Pooja is sent from Kathmandu to solve the case. But when she arrives, the brewing political unrest and violent protests throw her off course, and she is forced to seek help from Mamata, a local Madhesi policewoman. By putting aside systemic discrimination and pushing through everyday misogyny, the women solve the case - but at what personal cost?

title: Eklo-I
plot: In the aftermath of a mysterious virus outbreak in 2030, humanity fled Earth. By 2090, the sole surviving human lives in the mountains of Nepal. When two astronauts, having settled in space, return to Earth, they inadvertently encounter this last human survivor. As they delve into the mountain mysteries, they uncover the chilling enigma of the zombies that now roam the land.

title: Jaari 2: Song of Chyabrung
plot: Continuing the story of the first film, Jaari 2 follows the journey of Namsang and his wife Hangma as they embark on a new chapter of their married life. The film's narrative is deeply rooted in the culture of the Limbu community and revolves around the Chyabrung, a traditional drum that symbolizes love, identity and resilience. The film explores how Namsang navigates personal struggles and the complexities of his community's traditions with the guidance of the drum's rhythms.

title: Mantra
plot: Mantra is a fictitious love story of himalaya about Ghyappo (Cowboy as Saugat Malla) and a Girl Sangmo (Diya Pun) and his struggle to get her love.

title: Tel Visa
plot: A Nepalese family navigate cultural differences in the US, and the complications social media can cause. Despite their struggles and fears, the family support each other. The question of what 'normal' is subjective to the culture.

title: Missing: Keti Harayeko Suchana
plot: A gripping tale of two young people from contrasting worlds who are thrust together by a twist of fate. A man from Nepal's Terai plains accidentally abducts a woman from the northern hills, bringing her to his rural village in Janakpur. What begins as a clash of personalities slowly transforms into a journey of shared understanding, bridging deep-seated social and cultural divides.

title: Goodbye Sisters
plot: The journey begins in vibrant, spiritual yet harsh Kathmandu and continues towards the majestic Himalayas. Twenty-one-year-old Jamuna accompanies her younger sister on what could be their last trip to the mountains.

title: White Sun
plot: Young Pooja lives with her mother in a village in Nepal. Though saddened by the death of her grandfather, she is secretly thrilled at the prospect of meeting the man she hopes may be her father — Chandra, a former Maoist guerrilla who is returning home after a decade-long civil conflict.

title: Pitambar
plot: Pitambar is a story of a fearless and tactful warrior who has fought many hardships. Being a rejected child at an early age, he got the chance to be intimate with the roller coaster of life, making him like a rock. He wants to destroy the empire of perpetrators in Parasi to regain his mom's prestigious image but discovers the shocking truths one after the other in his way. The circumstances are against him but his mother is always there to embrace, to lead, and to support him.

title: Degree Maila: M.A. 3rd Class
plot: Degree Maaila is the only English graduate in a village, who has returned from a city. As the campus committee is warned to run the English classes properly or bear the consequences of shutting down, they decide to hire him for the job, which he declines. Maaila's love interest and the committee want him to take up the job but he is adamant.

title: Abhimanyu: Chapter 1
plot: The gripping social thriller that exposes the brutal reality of dowry culture through a suspenseful narrative filled with twists, emotions, and a race for justice

title: Hami Teen Bhai
plot: Film about three brothers.

title: Bhuthan: The World’s Happiest Man
plot: Bhutan: The World’s Happiest Man tells the story of Mr. Bishnu, an aging Bhutanese-American in Ohio, who dreams of returning to his homeland. After his unexpected death, his family faces a conflict over his final resting place, torn between past memories and present realities. This emotional drama explores themes of love, loss, and the search for belonging.

title: Narasimha Avatar
plot: Set in Kathmandu, Narsimha Avatar is an action thriller in which a feared gangster’s empire is challenged by a mysterious masked vigilante, while a disgraced cop seeking revenge and a young man haunted by a hidden past become entangled in a web of crime, justice, and destiny.

title: Himalayan Gold
plot: A young village girl's journey to collect the rare Himalayan herb Yarsagumba turns into a desperate fight for survival when she encounters a ruthless gang of robbers in the mountains.

title: Shape of Momo
plot: Bishnu returns to her Himalayan village after quitting her job, only to face mounting family pressures and societal expectations. As tensions rise with her pregnant sister's arrival and a budding relationship with a "suitable" boy from her community, Bishnu must choose between conforming to tradition or claiming her independence.

title: The Black Hen
plot: We are in the year 2001, a temporary ceasefire brings a much-needed break to a small war-torn village in Northern Nepal, bringing much joy among the residents. Prakash and Kiran, two young close friends, are also starting to feel the change in the air. Though they are divided by caste and social creed, they remain inseparable, and start raising a hen given to Prakash by his sister, with hopes to save money by selling her eggs. However, the hen goes missing. To find it, they embark on a journey, innocently unaware of the tyranny brought by the fragile ceasefire.

title: Kabaddi 4: The Final Match
plot: After the events of Kabaddi 3, Kaji no longer seeks love as he suffers from the pain of his previous relationships.

title: Ek Bhagavad Ra Ek Geeta
plot: Ek Bhagavad Ra Ek Gita highlights the struggles and hypocrisy faced by transgender people in Nepalese society, where they are sought for blessings yet denied dignity and respect.

title: Ama Khando
plot: Khando is 26-years-old, impoverished and illiterate, toiling the harvest away in other people’s farms in exchange for buckwheat to feed her 7-year-old son Dhondup. How could Khando be a ‘Good Mother’?

title: The Twelve Thousand
plot: At 13 years old, Sona was sold from Nepal into forced-prostitution in India. She’s not the only one—an estimated 12,000 children are trafficked out of Nepal every year. The Twelve Thousand is a dramatic short film depicting the true story of one trafficked survivor.

title: Babari
plot: A girl hires a local boy to accompany her to meet her boyfriend but things take a turn.

title: The Mirage
plot: During the period of civil war, in the countryside of Nepal, a curious  boy follows his mother to his uncle's house. In this poetic journey, he  witnesses various group of people trying to pursue liberation in their  own ways.

title: Kathaputali : The Puppet
plot: Trapped in an abandoned royal palace, a young prince must confront the unsettling secrets of his family’s past, escape evil forces and battle dark magic in order to survive.

title: Lalibazar
plot: An enigmatic woman navigates the Lalibazar, clutching a child she intends to offer as a tribute to the powerful Kaji. This dark social thriller explores a world of high-stakes exploitation where innocence is the only currency strong enough to buy the favor of the elite.

title: Farki Farki
plot: Based on a time 'infinite time loop' theory, a light hearted romantic comedy that showcases various aspects of our lives.

title: Dui Nambari
plot: As two friends get into hunting for a living in a small village in rural Nepal, their lives take a turn when a Nepali-speaking Bhutanese woman arrives there.

title: Cha Ekan Cha
plot: Cha Ekan Cha is a 2013 Nepali comedy action film written and co-produced by Deepak Raj Giri and directed by Dinesh D.C..It includes a cast of Nepali television comedians like Deepak Raj Giri, Jeetu Nepal, Sitaram Kattel and Kedar Ghimire, this ought to be an outrageous and funny affair. However, with all the talents involved, the film makes no effort in offering anything substantial that would’ve complemented the already established brilliance of these people. It’s a film that is in dire search of a clear plot and stretches so far to feature length only through jokes and one-liners. Cha Ekan Cha received positive to mixed reviews from critics upon release. The film has been praised for its direction, cinematography, action sequences, exotic locations.

title: Pension Patta
plot: Directed and written by Maotse Gurung, *Pension Patta* (2026) is a Nepali social drama starring Dayahang Rai, Sunil Thapa, Pashupati Rai, Gaumaya Gurung, Buddhi Tamang, and Wilson Bikram Rai. Produced under the banner of Maotse Gurung Films, the narrative centers on human emotions and family relationships within contemporary Nepali society. While the title alludes to the lifestyle of an ex-soldier (*Lahure*), the core plot delves into the personal transformation of a greedy young protagonist who gradually learns the value of generosity and compassion.

title: Julebi
plot: Julebi is a Nepali film directed by Ganeshdev Pandey that intertwines three distinct stories centered around the complexities of sexual psychology and modern relationships. Set in Ratnapark, the film explores the desires and passions of seven interconnected characters, with a particular focus on the experiences of Nepali women. The narrative delves into the lives of a sex worker, portrayed by Suraksha Pant, and other characters navigating strained relationships and unspoken love. 𝐉𝐮𝐥𝐞𝐛𝐢 explores love born of choice and necessity. A tale of hearts entangled in the complexities of life, where love knows no one path.  The story of the movie revolves around the relationships of seven characters, interconnected and revolving like a 𝘑𝘶𝘭𝘦𝘣𝘪 (Nepali Sweet).

title: Naami: The Crisis Queen
plot: A short film that explores the harsh realities of youth moving away from their villages with dreams of a better future, only to become entangled in criminal activities and drug addiction.

title: Aa Bata Aama 2
plot: Serving as a sequel to the successful initial film, this drama continues to explore heartfelt themes of a mother's unconditional love, family sacrifices, and the intense emotional burdens young adults face when navigating home life and the pressure to move abroad.

title: I am Jit Bahadur
plot: Jit Bahadur’s journey from a struggling dreamer to a celebrated actor reveals the harsh price of fame, as his relentless ambition gradually distances him from the very family that once supported him.

title: Ram Naam Satya
plot: A high-stakes story with the theme of justice, retribution, and conflict, characterized by intense action sequences.

title: Sano Mann
plot: Saniya, an introverted woman leaves for Kathmandu, where she meets Nirvaan— a terminally ill but happy-go-lucky man who changes her outlook on life.

title: Fulbari
plot: In a small village of Ilam, Jaya Narayan Sharma and Laxmi Sharma are living their retired life. Meanwhile, their children who are living in the city are more focused on their own life and career. Living own life arise a problem in the family members which creates distancing when they are in need of each other.

title: Dorii
plot: Dorii tells the gripping story of a troubled young man abandoned by his father and burdened with debt. In his desperate search for wealth, greed takes over, leading him into the risky world of rope smuggling. His choices soon drag him into danger, with consequences that could change his life forever.

title: Mero Paisa Khoi ?
plot: A series of awkward and comical events ensue when several people set out to recover the money owed to them.

title: Jwai Saab
plot: Disheartened by his home life, Gobardhan's fate takes a turn when he hears an advertisement on the radio offering the chance to become a live-in son-in-law. After winning several competitions with great effort, Gobardhan finally succeeds in becoming a live-in son-in-law. However, his life changes when his relationship with his wife takes a new turn.

title: Jhola: 'Sati' an inhumane tradition
plot: After her husband's death, a girl is forced to die due to the Sati practice in Nepal.

title: Dimag Kharab
plot: When bureaucratic barriers obstruct Indra Sharma's desire to work overseas, his dreams of a stable family life take a turbulent turn.

title: Pahuna: The Little Visitors
plot: Three Nepalese children rely on each other for survival after they become separated from their parents while entering India.

title: Khusma
plot: Set in the backdrop and the aftermath of Maoist Insurgency— Khusma endures separation from her husband and awaits his return, only to resign herself to her fate.

title: Karsang
plot: A financially struggling village shaman finds himself torn between his obligations and his son's concerns for an animal.

title: Khorlo
plot: A woman traverses a harsh, snow-covered desert in heavy boots, pulling a suitcase. On her journey, she meets a man who woos her and promises to love her forever. Right after she trusts him, she is left heartbroken by his sudden absence. She continues her journey. Next, she meets a soldier, who tells her to wipe off her tears and then teaches to use the sword to kill off anything that looks likes love. She is filled with anger now and continues on her journey. She meets a monk, who reminds her of what a soft delicate flower-like she was before ‘life’ happened to her. Will she use the sword to fight off? For the monk looks a lot like love.

title: Family Man
plot: During a storm in a remote Nepalese village, a mysterious man finds refuge in the house of a family haunted by their father's disappearance years before. Strangely unwilling to leave, he slowly reawakens lost memories of the missing father.

title: Looking for a Lady with Fangs and a Moustache
plot: When an ambitious, skeptical young entrepreneur faces strange portents of impending death, he is thrust into a mystical world of ancient wisdom and traditional beliefs that points to a special woman as his only hope for life.

title: Dobato
plot: Dobato is a classic Nepali movie released in 2007, celebrated for bringing together some of the biggest icons of the Nepali film industry.

title: RUDRA
plot: Five boys gather one night and decide to pull up a prank on someone's father. Things go wrong when the prank turns into a nightmare.

title: Ke Ghar Ke Dera
plot: Ke Ghar Ke Dera  is a comedy-drama film directed by Dipendra K. Khanal and written by Singhe Lama and Mahesh Dawadi. The film is produced by Sharad Shrestha, Rajeeb Shrestha, Kiran Maharjan under the banner of A Plus Entertainment.

title: My Share of Sky: Ek Mutthi Badal
plot: A family drama exploring relationships, expectations, and changes across generations.

title: The Secrets of Radha
plot: Radha, a young pregnant woman from a village in Nepal, embarks on a journey to find her husband, a migrant worker in Doha, who's gone missing, but  instead discovers an intriguing web of lies, deceit and fraud behind his disappearance and of many others.

title: Journey of a Red Fridge
plot: This is a story of a 17-year-old boy named Hari Rai, who lives in a small village in the Himalayan Mountains of Nepal, and his extraordinary journey. Hari is a student. However, he also works as a porter so that he could pay for his tuition and cover his living expenses. Although very young, he already has three years of experience carrying loads up and down the mountain, mostly tourists' backpacks. This time, he gets a job to carry a huge red refrigerator from the top of the mountain, to the nearest town. We follow Hari Rai on his journey through the fascinating Himalayan landscapes, we discover Hariâs inner life, his thoughts, hopes and dreams and we also get to know the culture and the local peopleâs way of life in this region.

title: Asubh Ashtami
plot: A girl enters  nunnery and must sacrifice her hair signaling the end of her innocence.

title: Elephants in the Fog
plot: In a small Nepalese village nestled in the heart of a forest populated by wild elephants, Pirati is the matriarch of a Kinnar community. She dreams of escaping to a “normal” life with the man she is in love with. But when one of her daughters disappears, she must investigate and choose between love and responsibility to her community.

title: Bhagya Bidhata
plot: A superhit Nepali movie starring Rajesh Hamal and Nikhil Upreti.

title: Thooli
plot: Thooli is a 2014 Nepali drama film directed by Everest Surya Bohara. It highlights rural Nepali life and focuses on social themes, revolving around issues of exploitation and rigid societal norms.

title: Lakhey
plot: Sameer Maharjan is a bank manager. He regards Lakhey as Lord Indra and the protector of the community. His professional and family life is ruined as he gets trapped in the bank fraud case. Then he devises an increasingly perilous series of revenge tactics.

title: Rangeli
plot: A gritty gangster drama that explores themes of power, loyalty, and betrayal in the Rangeli region of the Terai plains.

title: What grows in Thari Maila’s vegetable garden?
plot: Thari Maila and his young wife are a childless couple residing in a secluded part of a village. The seemingly naive couple live off a lush vegetable garden but there's something beneath the surface that keeps the young wife in turmoil.

title: A Road to the Village
plot: Maila, a basket weaver, his wife Maile, and seven-year-old son Bindray live a simple village life in the mountainous region of eastern Nepal. When a road to their village is finally built, they see it as a blessing. Soon, along with the first bus, soda, television, and hip hop beats arrive. But as modern amenities creep in, Maila's life begins to unravel. He's trapped between sticking to tradition at home or going beyond the village to survive.Meanwhile Bindray uses his resourcefulness to get closer to the gifts the the new road brings. Ultimately Maila and Maile must figure out how to continue in a world that is leaving them behind.

title: Sorry Mom
plot: SORRY MOM is about a young brilliant student and his movement towards drugs. How his family, friends, and other circles get destroyed after his drug addiction. The brave mother never gave up and helped her son revive. The movie shows how the young school children unknowingly dipped towards drug addiction, and gives a good lesson showing its effect while providing real stories of the youth.

title: Thamel Dot Com
plot: One cool guy's life.

title: Jaari
plot: The central characters of Jaari are Namsang and Hangma, a married Limbu couple. After a brief argument with her spouse Namsang, Hangma chooses to leave him by giving back his Yuparung (a sacred coin). She then marries a different man after falling in love with him. The jaari is claimed by Namsang and his family, but Hangma’s family refuses to pay, forcing Hangma into slavery in Namsang’s household until the jaari is paid.

title: A Place Under the Sun
plot: A trio of best friends host a barbecue before moving on to the next chapters of their lives. However, the party is cut short when a stranger invites himself in, as secrets unfold.

title: Bhaire
plot: Two friends set out on a journey to find their long-lost childhood friend on behalf of his 84-year-old dying father. Along the journey, everyone goes through innumerable highs and lows defining love, struggle, care, conflict, and hope.

title: The Red Suitcase
plot: A pick-up truck driver leaves Kathmandu airport for a two-day drive with a delivery to a remote mountain village. On the high road, a solitary figure slowly makes his way, wheeling a small red suitcase toward the same village.

title: Mansarra
plot: Mansarra is a specific species of rice. According to legend, it doesn't sprout alone; its seeds are typically mixed with other rice species before planting. A couple’s marriage is tested by fertility issues and by the husband’s growing bond with a single mother and her son.

title: Flash Back (Farkera Herda)
plot: A boy comes back to Nepal to attend a wedding. However, he meets a village girl and instantly falls in love with her.

title: Estai Raicha
plot: A man refuses to give up on a love that the universe keeps trying to erase.

title: Gunyo Cholo: A Girl Coming of Age Story
plot: Gulabi is a transwoman groomed by her patriarchal father to join the military. When her father disowns her, Gulabi ends up living a life of prostitution in Kathmandu.

title: Pahaad
plot: The story about resilience of people living in the mountains of Nepal, navigating survival and hardship.

title: Anjila
plot: The movie "Anjila" is based on the real-life story of Anjila Tumbapo Subba, the captain and number one goalkeeper of Nepal's national women's football team. The film showcases her journey from being confined within her home to becoming the team captain, highlighting her struggles and determination .Anjila herself plays the lead role, with Dayahang Rai and Srijana Subba portraying her parents The movie emphasizes the importance of perseverance and the belief that with determination, nothing is impossible.

title: Appa
plot: Appa is a film based on a father son relationship told in the most simplest of manner. It delves into the world of Birkhey (Dayahang Rai) and Sid (Siddhant Raj Tamang) as they explore their relationship. It further deepens into a teenage romance with Kavya (Allona Kabo Lepcha).

title: अन्त्येष्टि - Antyesthi
plot: Set in rural Makawanpur, this powerful family drama unfolds during the 13 days of a funeral ritual that brings an entire family under one roof. Clouded by the grief and nostalgia, will the family stay together or death tear them apart!

title: Dukhi Aatma
plot: Dukhi Aatma follows a struggling poet whose love life is shaken by erectile dysfunction. Desperate for a cure, he tries doctors, herbs, and even spiritual methods. As his efforts fail, he faces emotional turmoil, questioning his identity, masculinity, and the fragility of love in the face of silent struggles.

title: Ningwasum
plot: Ningwasum follows two time travellers Miksam and Mingsoma, played by Subin Limbu and Shanta Nepali respectively, in the Himalayas weaving indigenous folk stories, culture, climate change and science fiction.

title: Gauthali
plot: A young girl forced into an early marriage is denied her education and trapped with an unstable husband, but she uses school as a form of resistance to reclaim her future and challenge oppressive community traditions.

title: Reemai
plot: Reemai is the story of a young woman who refuses a childhood marriage arranged under traditional Gurung customs, and must face the consequences of her decision. The film beautifully captures the essence of Gurung culture, exploring themes of love, tradition, and personal freedom

title: Outlaw - Dafa 219
plot: A Nepali action packed movie starring Sushil Shrestha & Directed by Bickee Maharjan.

title: Hajar Juni Samma
plot: It's about a man and his journey of searching for the lost love. His journey will take you from the city of lakes that is pokhara to the traveler's paradise Sikkim. Fighting the obstacles , will he finally end his search or his search will end his spirit. Only time will tell...

title: Kamala Miss
plot: The story inspired from the classic drama by Bijaya Malla's 'Kohi Kina Barbad Hos' exploring the themes of child custody, the effect of love, maternity effects, and guardian’s obligation.

title: Balidan
plot: In the remote corner of the country lies Bhilmaghat, a village that looks like paradise from the outside. Beneath its beauty hides a kingdom of exploitation and fear ruled by a hypocritical priest “Baba,” who preys on innocents in the name of faith. A boy named Abhay survives a horrific ritual sacrifice with the help of Bhavani, who plants the seed of revolt—only to lose her husband in a direct clash with Baba. Years later, Abhay struggles to save his ailing mother but is ensnared in a sinister plot to bomb a massive temple inauguration and divide the nation. Now, he must save his mother, protect thousands of innocents, and end the tyranny. If he fails, the sacrifice will be his own. If he succeeds, his balidan will be remembered for generations.

title: The Silent Echo
plot: Four teenagers, from a remote mountain village, spend their days in an abandoned bus on a ridge, playing music. When a band competition is organized in a neighbouring town, they gather money and courage to be part of it

title: Ram Balaram
plot: A story depicting the conflict of of two brothers— Ram and Balaram.

title: Mayabi
plot: The story follows Joy, a cheerful and kind person, who falls in love with Arya, a doctor influenced by traditional values. As their bond grows, they face many challenges that put their love to the test. The film explores whether they can overcome these obstacles and confess their love before time runs out.

title: Unko Sweater
plot: A tender romantic drama set in the serene hills of eastern Nepal. The film explores the quiet bond between Dharanidhar Kafle, a reserved young man from a traditional Brahmin family, and Phool, a spirited girl from the Gurung community.

title: Hijo Aja Ka Kura
plot: Three young people decide on choosing to emigrate for better lives, but their families say otherwise.

title: Aadhi Bato
plot: Aadhi Bato is a traingular love story nepali movie featuring Jenisha KC, Yash Kumar (singer), Sabin Shrestha, Anu Shah and Ruby Rana in main roles. Aadhi Bato movie is shot mostly in kathmandu and Kalinchowk with some scenes of UK as well.

title: Hrashwo Deergha
plot: Hrashwo Deergha is an action-comedy directed by Chandra Pant, starring Neeta Dhungana, Harihar Adhikari, Bipana Thapa, Brahmanandam, Pradeep Singh Rawat, and Mohammad Ali.

title: Nikhil Dai
plot: A classical famous Nepali movie.

title: Bato: Road to Death
plot: Paramedic Komal is on a mission to deliver a live heart case for transplant. Tony drives taxis, his latest passenger is filmmaker Manjil, on his way to Dharan after hearing a tragic news about his mother. Their journey takes a dark turn when they encounter a vigilant cult with intentions to consume the hearts of young women in order to be awakened.

title: Hostel 3
plot: The film follows the lives of teenagers living in a hostel, capturing their fun, struggles, and personal growth. The story revolves around a group of students navigating their way through school life, friendships, and challenges.

title: Paaniphoto
plot: An old couple refuses to perform the last rites of their only son at the last minute and is determined to look for him, after their son’s best friend, who disappeared along with him, returns home after 15 years.

title: Viral Gorkhey
plot: Delves into the complexities of fame, culture, and connection, weaving a compelling tale set against Nepal's breathtaking landscapes.

title: Roll No. 1
plot: A story of a boy struggling to fulfill his father's dream of winning a scholarship from the government.

title: Mahajatra
plot: Mahajatra unfolds the gripping tale of three men, bound by a tumultuous past, entrusted with the clandestine duty of concealing illicit funds. The film delves into the challenges, triumphs, and setbacks faced by these individuals as they navigate the complexities of safeguarding their entrusted task.

title: The Man from Kathmandu
plot: A disaffected American-Nepali half Hindu-Muslim boy detours to Kathmandu on his way to join the on going war in Syria, but his battle against a local don who kills his grandfather teaches him to follow a far more greater purpose in life, and in the process he reconnects with his roots, culture and traditions of the Himalayan nation, which he had forgotten.

title: In Between the Lines
plot: A woman navigates the line between her sick mother and her secret lover.

title: Blood Money
plot: Based on a real story, the film depicts the agonizing experience of a migrant worker overseas and the harsh realities his family faces back in Nepal. It heavily criticizes societal prejudices against the wives of migrant workers and exposes the dark side of politics surrounding foreign employment.

title: Bandaids
plot: The young son of a street vendor is put to work at a factory after city police confiscate his father’s cart. As people around him grow increasingly harsh, he learns that it is easier to take than to ask.

title: Mahabhoj
plot: Mahabhoj is a social drama set in the backdrop of Nepal’s political system. It tells the story of everyday people who stand up against those who exploit them for selfish interests. The film highlights themes like rebellion, justice, and the struggle against political corruption and social inequality.  At its core, the story follows a bank loan manager in urgent need of money. When he turns to his father for help, he learns that his father’s lifelong savings have been stolen by a corrupt cooperative and a group of greedy individuals. This discovery sets off a powerful journey of resistance and truth.

title: Bishwas
plot: विश्वास (Bishwas) is a heart-wrenching tale of one man's journey from rural simplicity to the complexity of city life. The protagonist, a humble villager, leaves behind his mother and the only life he's ever known to earn enough money to pay for her medical treatment.  As his search for money leads him down a dangerous path, he realizes the true cost of his quest and the sacrifices he must make. With betrayal and danger lurking at every turn, the protagonist must find the strength within himself to overcome the odds and save not only his mother, but his own soul.  With a powerful blend of drama, romance, and adventure, "विश्वास" is a timeless story of one man's journey towards redemption and self-discovery.

title: Devi
plot: Devi's life was upended following her arrest during Nepal's civil war accused of rebellion. Tortured and raped while in custody, she faced depression and isolation. Despite hardship, Devi joined rebel forces and became a prominent member of Parliament.

title: Shree Bukuro : The Home Calling
plot: A heartfelt family drama about returning to ancestral village and rediscovering the meaning of home through reconnection with family and the mountains, the story shows how embracing one’s roots can heal relationships and bring true clarity in life.

title: Pheri Bhetaula
plot: A Nepali romantic film.

title: The Other Side of the Coin
plot: Three immigrant workers in a basement navigate the heavy costs of global labor.

title: The Final Conversation
plot: A psychological short film exploring a young man's imagined final conversation with his mother. Shot in black and white, it delves into themes of grief, memory, and emotional closure. Directed by Prashanna Mali, the film has received international acclaim, including an award at the World Film Festival in Cannes.

title: Mugalan
plot: Three friends faith is tested when they involve in foreign unemployment.

title: Colors of Evening
plot: An 11-year-old city boy sets out to buy alcohol for his father at the command of his mother.

title: Agastya – Chapter 1
plot: Saurya is haunted by a difficult past and grapples with his troubled mind. After a year, his brother embarks on a quest to find him. A touching story unfolds as Nisan delves into Saurya's history.

title: Basa Parkher
plot: I'm too busy to take care of my family.

title: Sanrakshan
plot: This movie tells the story of the politics of Nepal how they use their power to control whole nation.

title: Chapali Height 3
plot: The third installment of Chapali Height movie.

title: Sarangi : A Journey of Purna Bahadur
plot: In the heart of a remote village, Purna Bahadur Ko Sarangi weaves a poignant drama about the unbreakable bond between a struggling father and his son. Purna Bahadur, a humble and impoverished musician, clings to the hope of a better life through his beloved sarangi, an instrument that carries the echoes of his dreams and sacrifices. As the family faces relentless hardship, the son, who yearns for a brighter future, becomes increasingly distant, torn between his aspirations and the harsh reality of their life.

title: Colour of Tea
plot: In the diverse village of Madhes in Nepal, Harilal, an elderly Dalit, goes to a tea shop to buy a glass of sweet tea from home. One day those wets are forgotten in the tea shop. The next day, when he reached the tea shop and asked for tea in his missing glass, Sahuji claimed that he did not miss the glass. And Harilal fights for his Geelas. Will Harilal able to find his missing glass?

title: Kamana
plot: Kamana goes back to her parents' house because her dowry isn’t paid. The film shows how the dowry system treats women as objects and makes them feel lost. Kamana wants to be free, but societal pressure keeps her from finding her true self.

title: Lori: Melancholy of my Mother's Lullabies
plot: A mother sings lullabies to her 12-years-old daughter in order to calm her down. But, when the lullabies end, and the daughter comes to her senses, the reality turns out to be much grimmer, and life-altering.

title: Invitation
plot: 10-year-old Tashi and his ageing grandma live as tenants to a wealthy family, in a remote mountain village in Darjeeling. Their job is to look after the family’s cardamom orchard and guard it at night from wild animals. An approaching wedding at their patron’s house excites Tashi. But will they invite him?

title: Bihe Pass
plot: When a shady married man is caught in the illegal act by his wife's father, a government official, he must pass the Public Service Commission exam or risk losing his wife.

title: Pashupati Prasad 2: Bhasme Don
plot: After the tragic death of Pashupati Prasad, Bhasme sets out in search of his own identity. He must complete his journey while also protecting his empire.

title: Batulo Jun
plot: Set three decades ago in a rural village, the film follows a simple country boy whose pure, old-fashioned love story clashes with the harsh realities and changing complexities of modern life.

title: Prasad 2
plot: A film about society, politics and relationships that starts with a couple who had an inter-caste love relationship and have returned to the village to face society with their 9-year-old son, Prasad. As the country now has entered the Republic system, the constitution was written in favour of the people & caste untouchability was made a crime by the law. The story revolves around the future conflict of Baburam, Narayani, who returned to the village, the struggle for that and an ups and downs journey. For them, what is greater than love?

title: Satidevi
plot: Kul Bahadur, a poor villager battling alcoholism, has four daughters. Though he wishes for a son, his eldest daughter, Sati Devi, strives to make him happy. However, their lives are marred by heart-wrenching events.

title: Jaar
plot: In Nepalese society, "Jaar" refers to a man who has an illicit affair with another man's wife. During the reign of Jung Bahadur Rana (1846–1877), he proclaimed that the husband had the right to behead the guilty Jaar. Rudraman and Thuli as outcasts, face a perilous journey, cut off from the security of family and tradition.

title: Sanglo
plot: The fight against the sinners and the culprits.

title: Rammita Koo Pirati
plot: Rammita emerges from a toxic five-year relationship with Sahaj , a relationship she once believed was her entire world. Heartbroken and more lost than ever, she convinces herself that rebounds will help her regain her strength and happiness. However, as she moves from one fleeting connection to another, her sense of self slowly begins to fade. She drifts away from her friendships, loses touch with her identity, and feels increasingly disconnected from who she truly is.

title: Dadyaa: The Woodpeckers of Rotha
plot: In their remote village, haunted by memories, Atimaley and Devi find themselves faced with a dilemma when a dear friend leaves without saying goodbye.

title: Prem Geet 3
plot: The epic story of the mighty khazak kingdom from the beyul of himalayas set on an ancient periodic setup. The story revolves around two major characters PREM and GEET, where prem is supposed to be the heir to the throne as he was born on the most auspicious time according to astrology. However he had to overcome one obstacle, that is Prem (love) itself.

title: Kushti
plot: A touching comedy-drama about a wrestling-loving couple torn by clashing personalities. They can't live together, yet can't stay apart. This story is a humorous, emotional journey of love, struggle, and deep connection.

title: Lakshya
plot: An action movie, 'Lakshya' is a presentation of Om Kiran Films. Boxing, dancing and fighting the mafia.

title: New Moon Rain
plot: Madan, a 55-year-old immigrant professor in the U.S., faces personal turmoil after a domestic dispute results in job loss and widespread gossip. Haunted by estrangement from his family and a deep yearning for his deceased parents, he finds a fleeting sense of solace in a dream visitation on a new moon night—just before his mysterious disappearance.

title: Mokshyam
plot: A businessman struggles to fix a deal for his startup while attending a temple ritual, thus ignoring his religious wife and their daughter, who leave heartbroken, after he aggressively rages at them. Meanwhile, a mysterious ascetic stalks them everywhere. When the businessman receives shocking news, an astonishing connection between him and the ascetic is revealed.

title: The Road to Til
plot: Disability rights advocate Chhitup Lama travels to Til Village in Nepal’s Humla district, to explore what happens when climate change impacts remote mountain communities.

title: Chha Maya Chhapakkai
plot: Chha Maya Chhapakkai is a 2019 Nepali social-comedy film directed by Dipendra Lama and written by Deepak Raj Giri. The story revolves around two families from opposing political backgrounds whose rivalry intensifies when their children fall in love. This conflict escalates into a political upheaval in their village, blending humor with social commentary.  The film stars Deepak Raj Giri, Keki Adhikari, Kedar Ghimire, and Jeetu Nepal, and it was both a critical and commercial success in Nepal, grossing over रू 9.4 crore at the box office

title: Six Strands
plot: Partially based on true stories, 'Six Strands' is a character study inspired by the world famous Darjeeling tea industry and its undisclosed secrets.

title: Naike
plot: What's wrong with a man dressing up? A movie with action, songs, and romance.

title: Maijharo
plot: A man’s desperate fight to save his father’s graveyard from a powerful businessman is complicated by the return of his former lover.

title: Jhingedaau
plot: The film portrays two sisters-in-law whose friendly rivalry during the traditional dice game "Jhinge Dau" leads to family discord, highlighting the unforeseen consequences of seemingly harmless actions.

title: When Glaciers Go
plot: A diminishing water supply is driving people from their land in a remote region of Nepal.
