The younger generation of the Gurung family adapts by commuting from their ancestral home, where subsistence depends on grazing goats and cows, to a village that has a commercial apple orchard, fed by irrigation. “We cannot give up cultivating our fields,” a elderly man explains. “The apple farm is not going to be able to feed us easily.” The older generation believes that water shortages stem from road building and bulldozing, upsetting the natural order, a young man explains. Both generations fly prayer flags, beseeching water.

title: Sijou
plot: Sijou is a small boy who lives in the village of Saikhonguri, a village in Baksa near the Indo-Bhutan border. He lives with his father. One day, his life takes a drastic shift when he becomes a victim of the Feudal Land Tenure System which was in practice in Bhutan till 1958. Gradually, his life takes drastic turns which eventually turn him into a monk.

title: Papi Manchhe
plot: The story of a family that is shattered by a sinful politician.

title: Shakti
plot: When her mischievous daughter falls mysteriously ill, a single mother is drawn into Kathmandu’s supernatural underworld of shamans and secrets— where resilience may be their only path to redemption.

title: Chandra
plot: With his elderly grandfather, seven year old Chandra walks through earthquake-hit Kathmandu to visit his mother and newborn sibling in hospital.

title: Tell me who I am
plot: In 1990, one sixth of the population of Bhutan was exiled because they demanded greater democratic rights from the ruler. The film follows the story of Sarita, a 13-year-old girl born in Khudunabari, a refugee camp in Nepal where over 100,000 Bhutanese exiles live. Now they will be "relocated": thousands of families will be forced to emigrate and their Lotshampa identity will disappear forever. Sarita and her friends will not tell us their odyssey: they will dance and sing it.

title: Punam
plot: Nine-year-old Punam Tamang, lives in Bhaktapur, Nepal. She lost her mother when she was five years old and since that time she has been the family caretaker to her small siblings while her father works from dawn till night. Punam tells us her story and she introduces us to her family, neighborhood, teachers, and friends.

title: Harry Ki Pyari
plot: Harry and his 'Pyaari' Jenny, are chasing similar goals in life, but the extent to which they both go about to achieve this is unimaginable.

title: AKTOR: Take One
plot: A middle-class boy, Kushal, dreams of becoming an actor and faces numerous challenges and transformative experiences as he strives to prove himself in the film industry.

title: Feri Resham Filili
plot: When Resham wins the US DV lottery, village tensions rise. Stranded before reaching America, he reunites with unreliable friend Hariya. Both chase dreams amid lies, jealousy and survival challenges.

title: Antim Sanskar: The Last Ritual
plot: A gifted but troubled detective investigates the disappearance of a celebrated filmmaker.

title: Mitjyu
plot: A story about two guy bonded with the relationship 'Mitjyu' yet navigating through the complexities with each other.

title: Senti Virus
plot: A political satire of Nepali Politics

title: Mirmire
plot: In a remote Himalayan village, Nima's father tells everyone her mother works abroad, a daily lie to shield his daughter from a painful truth buried in village gossip. As whispers intensify, he fights to preserve Nima's smile.

title: Rato Tika Nidharma
plot: Rato Tika Nidharma is a 2019 Nepali romantic comedy film. The film was directed and written by Ashok Sharma, and produced by Kiran Sharma, and Kriti Sharma. It stars Samragyee RL Shah, and Ankit Sharma in the lead roles.

title: Devwani
plot: A private college owner seeks revenge on a student after the student refuses to study at his college.

title: Tattini: The Moon Is Bright Tonight
plot: A dead body of a migrant worker arrives in a village of stone workers in southern part of Nepal. TATTINI, his recently wed wife and now widow, plans to escape and use the money from his insurance to start a new life but her father-in-law is determined to stop her from leaving with the insurance money of his dead son.

title: The Lost Years
plot: Around 1990, a young boy from Jhapa (Eastern Nepal) came to Kathmandu to weave Nepali carpets. After spending some time in Kathmandu, given that he is underage, the boy is laid off from his job. Some people from the Northern Gorkha found him. Assuring him a job as a cowherd, he was taken to Tsum Valley after a two-week walk. From there on, he never returned home from the Mountain.

title: Maya
plot: Maya, Bikram and Kancha suddenly find themselves trapped in the heart of Kathmandu. Vulnerable, penniless and alone, they are forced to live their life on the streets with only hopes and dreams of happiness.

title: Koshedhunga
plot: A strong-willed woman defies her father in order to wed her childhood sweetheart, but she faces more antagonism when her new husband leaves the country to find work.

title: Upahaar
plot: Upahaar-The Journey of Life is a Nepali movie about a young entrepreneur's journey of ambition, resilience, and triumphs. The movie is a rollercoaster of emotions, with moments of laughter and tears.

title: Chhakka Panja 4
plot: Nirvaya Singh is currently the head of one of Nepal's most influential political parties, while his son Dik Vijay is deeply involved in criminal operations. When a political election is going to take place, Nirvaya becomes paralyzed, and Dik Vijay assumes control as president.

title: Harishchandra
plot: Harishchandra  is a 1951 Indian Nepali-language film based on the story of the legendary king Harischandra from Hindu mythology. This is credited as the first Nepali language film however, many don't acknowledge as so, since all of the production and filming was in India.

title: Chapali Height 2
plot: Chapali Height 2 is a Nepali psychological thriller film directed by Dipendra K. Khanal and produced by Arjun Kumar. The film stars Avash Shrestha, Paramita Rana and Mariska Pokharel. It is the sequel to the 2012 film Chapali Height.

title: Mero Euta Saathi Chha
plot: Jay, who is the heir to the property, is raised by his grandparents after the death of his parents. Being the rich guy he abuses his wealth. One day he skips class and while riding on his bike, he gets challenged from another bike rider in black helmet. He gets ahead of him and blocks his way. He acknowledges that the black helmet person was chased by Nepal Police for crime of robbery.

title: Parbati
plot: Autism is not a weakness or a flaw, it is simply a different way of experiencing the world. Every person learns, thinks, feels, communicates, and understands life in their own unique way. This story follows the emotional journey of a brother and sister whose deep bond becomes the heart of the film. Amid society’s misunderstandings and everyday struggles, their relationship reflects love, acceptance, sacrifice, and the strength of family, creating a touching and inspiring tale about embracing individuality.

title: KATHPUTALI: The Angel of the Himalayas
plot: Leading Nepalese film scholar and Tokyo University of Information Sciences professor Ito Toshiaki serves as the first Japanese director and screenplay writer for a Nepalese film in this mid-length feature film. Screened in Kathmandu and recipient of the National Film Award from the Nepal government and the Nepal Short Film Critic's Award.  A couple visits a mountain village in Nepal to put on a puppet theater performance. A young boy named Ramesh is brought to the festival being held in the village by his grandma Maya. There, he meets a girl named Nisha who wears the same ethnic attire as his grandmother.

title: Life Dyamage
plot: In a world where gender roles are suddenly reversed due to a divine mistake, three individuals navigate relationships, identity, and societal expectations.

title: Portrait of Grief
plot: A small excerpt from the scenes of a complicated relationship between a mother and her daughter.

title: Malati Mangale
plot: Malati and Mangales navigate love, sacrifice, and life's harsh realities, where emotions clash with responsibilities, revealing the fragile yet enduring bond that keeps them together through pain and change.

title: 2.0 Chhadke
plot: Family of Mendog is killed by Gangsters of Manang while collecting Yarsagumba, but he lives to plot his vengence.

title: Sapoot
plot: Sapoot is a notable Nepali drama film released under the direction of Yogesh Ghimire. It is well-regarded for its emotional storyline and features a popular cast of iconic actors in the Nepali film industry.

title: Taraharu
plot: Mohan is an honest Transport company worker living with his wife Maiya. Their peaceful life is disrupted by Ratan, a powerful and immoral transport company manager who becomes obsessed with Maiya. Using Mohan’s financial struggles to his advantage, Ratan pressures him and eventually gets him fired. Despite facing betrayal, theft, and police trouble, Mohan sinks into depression and nearly gives up on life. With the support of Jyami an occasionally made friend and band owner, other friends, and his passion for music, he regains hope. In the end, Mohan succeeds in opening his café. Ratan’s crimes are exposed, and the story concludes with the message that life is worth living.

title: Ghatak 'The Thriller'
plot: Ghatak 'The Thriller' is a Nepali movie released on February 15, 2008. It is an action-thriller directed by Ramji Lamichhane and produced by Prabhu Shamsher Jung Bahadur Rana.

title: Mummy
plot: After the tragic loss of their beloved family dog, a daughter adopts a mysterious black cat, unwittingly inviting dark forces that threaten to tear her family apart.

title: Jhingedaau 2
plot: The movie serves as a sequel focusing on family dynamics and cultural conflicts within a traditional household, tracking how greed and misunderstandings test the deep emotional bonds between brothers and their wives.

title: Hunter's Moon
plot: After a successful season in the Upper Dhauladhar Mountains, a hunter hides his meat in a cave and heads home with his first load. At home, his wife is upset over his prolonged absence and its impact on their disabled daughter. He promises to return quickly and the next morning, sets out to retrieve the rest of the meat and leave the forest for the season. To his dismay, he finds the cave empty and the meat stolen. Using his hunter instincts he launches a desperate search through the  treacherous mountain, discovering that the rival is just as cunning. In this quest for meat, humans behave no differently than wild animals. Battling harsh conditions and difficult terrains, they go to all extremes to prove themselves stronger and more deserving. This allegorical tale of conflicting morals and twisted values reveals the true nature of man in the wild.

title: when light goes and drakness comes
plot: old aged couple faces existential crisis and hopelessness.

title: Chameli Ko Poi
plot: A successful Londoner is completely ruined and left homeless when his estranged Nepali wife tracks him down to exploit their un-divorced status.
